message.content: acquaint

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

[package]
name = "vc-x1"
version = "0.42.0-4"
edition = "2024"

[dependencies]
chrono = { version = "0.4", default-features = false, features = ["now"] }
clap = { version = "4.6", features = ["derive", "wrap_help"] }
clap_complete = { version = "4.6", features = ["unstable-dynamic"] }
jj-lib = "0.40"
log = { version = "0.4", features = ["std"] }
pollster = "0.4"

[target.'cfg(unix)'.dependencies]
libc = "0.2"
# vc-config: Vibe Coding workspace configuration
#
# workspace-path is this repo's path relative to the workspace root.
# Used to resolve changeID paths in git trailers (e.g. ochid: /changeID).
# other-repo is the relative path to the counterpart repo.

[workspace]
path = "/"
other-repo = ".claude"

108	   both sides in one place). Updates to `vc-x1 init` / `clone`
109	   needed for the new layout.

112	   code refs to designs; codify in CLAUDE.md alongside the

116	1. Per-line/per-thread runtime log points (future, maybe) [36]

   shared `CommonArgs` change picks all four up). Drops

1. Consider renaming the `.vc-config.toml` `[workspace]`
   section. Rust readers expect `[workspace]` to mean a

   Candidates: `[repo-list]`, `[project]`, `[dual-repo]`.
   Breaking change — needs migration story (read both

1. Add "::" revision syntax for jj compatibility

1. Add integration tests in tests/ for subcommands using temp jj repos (tempfile crate)
1. Fix .claude repo history: dev0 through dev2 sessions squashed into wrong commit [4],[5]
1. Add `vc-x1 setup` subcommand: completions install, .claude repo init, symlink setup [27]
1. Add dynamic revision completion via `ArgValueCompleter` (jj doesn't complete revsets either) [28],[29]

## Done

Completed tasks are moved from `## Todo` to here, `## Done`, as they are completed
and older `## Done` sections are moved to [done.md](done.md) to keep this file small.

- Pre-commit checklist requires `--locked` for `cargo install` (0.41.0-1) [71]
- Scope continuation: sync --scope (0.41.0-2) [71]
- Scope continuation: capture --scope-everywhere direction (0.41.0-3) [71]
- Scope continuation: capture --scope enum vocabulary (0.41.0-4) [71]
- Scope continuation: cycle close-out — push/finalize work deferred to 0.42.0 (0.41.0) [71]

1	# Notes
2	
3	This directory contains various notes and documentation related to the project.
4	Each file is organized by topic for easy reference.
5	
6	By default there are chores-*.md and todo.md. Chores are general notes
7	about tasks and todo.md contains short term tasks and their status.
8	
9	In the future we I expect we may want to create a "notes"
10	database to better manage the information, TBD.
11	
12	Examples chore file:
13	```
14	# Chores-01.md
15	 
16	General maintenance tasks and considerations for the project see other files for
17	more specific topics. A chore in a chores file provides quick information on the
18	how and why of a particular chore.
19	
20	## Create a binary that lists jj info 
21	
22	This binary should list the changeID, commitID, and description title
23	and using `jj-lib`
24	```
25	
26	## Workflow and conventions
27	
28	Bot-facing workflow, versioning, and code conventions live in
29	[`../CLAUDE.md`](../CLAUDE.md). Start there for:
30	
31	- **Versioning during development** — single-step vs multi-step,
32	  `-N` pre-release suffixes, done-marker discipline.
33	- **Code Conventions** — doc comments on every file / fn / method,
34	  `// OK: …` justifications on `unwrap*` calls, ask-on-ambiguity,
35	  stuck detection.
36	- **Commit-Push-Finalize Flow** — two-checkpoint per-step
37	  discipline with hard stop after finalize.
38	
39	## Todo format
40	
41	Todo.md contains two main sections "Todo" and "Done" each item is a
42	short explanations of a tasks and links to more details using 1 or more
43	references.
44	
45	Todo items use lazy numbering — every entry begins with `1. ` and the
46	markdown renderer auto-numbers them. Reorder or insert items without
47	renumbering, and reference an entry by its displayed number ("let's
48	work on #3"). The Done section keeps `-` bullets — items aren't
49	referenced by number once completed.
50	
51	Multiple references must be separated: `[2],[3]` not `[2,3]` or `[2][3]`.
52	In markdown, `[2,3]` is a single ref key (won't resolve) and `[2][3]`
53	is parsed as display text `2` with ref key `3` (so `[2]` won't resolve).
54	
55	Examples:
56	
57	# Todo
58	- Add new feature X [details](features.md#feature-x)
59	- Fix bug Y [1]
60	
61	# Done
62	- Fixed issue Z [2],[3]
63	
64	[1]: bugs.md#bug-y
65	[2]: issues.md#issue-z
66	[3]: fixes.md#fix-z
67	
68	## Reference numbering
69	
70	Every note file (`todo.md`, `chores-NN.md`, `done.md`) keeps a
71	file-local `# References` section at the bottom. Reference numbers
72	are scoped to that file — `[1]` in `chores-07.md` and `[1]` in
73	`chores-01.md` are independent slots that may point at completely
74	different URLs. New chores files start their numbering at `[1]`.
75	
76	## Retiring Done entries
77	
78	`todo.md`'s `## Done` section is a rolling buffer of recently shipped
79	work, not a permanent log. Move entries into `done.md` at two natural
80	beats:
81	
82	- **Closing a ladder** — when the final `X.Y.Z` (no suffix) commit
83	  ships, decide which prior entries are no longer needed for nearby
84	  context and migrate them.
85	- **Opening a new ladder** — at `X.Y.Z-0`, do the same sweep before
86	  bumping the version.
87	
88	Migration mechanics:
89	
90	- Move the bullet itself from `todo.md > ## Done` to
91	  `done.md` (preserving the original ref number).
92	- Copy any references the moved entries cite into
93	  `done.md`'s `# References` section (those refs are file-local,
94	  so coexisting with `todo.md`'s namespace is fine).
95	- Prune any references in `todo.md > # References` no longer cited
96	  by anything in `## In Progress` / `## Todo` / `## Done`. This
97	  frees the numbers for future reuse.

file.content:
# Notes

This directory contains various notes and documentation related to the project.
Each file is organized by topic for easy reference.

By default there are chores-*.md and todo.md. Chores are general notes
about tasks and todo.md contains short term tasks and their status.

In the future we I expect we may want to create a "notes"
database to better manage the information, TBD.

Examples chore file:
```
# Chores-01.md
 
General maintenance tasks and considerations for the project see other files for
more specific topics. A chore in a chores file provides quick information on the
how and why of a particular chore.

## Create a binary that lists jj info 

This binary should list the changeID, commitID, and description title
and using `jj-lib`
```

## Workflow and conventions

Bot-facing workflow, versioning, and code conventions live in
[`../CLAUDE.md`](../CLAUDE.md). Start there for:

- **Versioning during development** — single-step vs multi-step,
  `-N` pre-release suffixes, done-marker discipline.
- **Code Conventions** — doc comments on every file / fn / method,
  `// OK: …` justifications on `unwrap*` calls, ask-on-ambiguity,
  stuck detection.
- **Commit-Push-Finalize Flow** — two-checkpoint per-step
  discipline with hard stop after finalize.

## Todo format

Todo.md contains two main sections "Todo" and "Done" each item is a
short explanations of a tasks and links to more details using 1 or more
references.

Todo items use lazy numbering — every entry begins with `1. ` and the
markdown renderer auto-numbers them. Reorder or insert items without
renumbering, and reference an entry by its displayed number ("let's
work on #3"). The Done section keeps `-` bullets — items aren't
referenced by number once completed.

Multiple references must be separated: `[2],[3]` not `[2,3]` or `[2][3]`.
In markdown, `[2,3]` is a single ref key (won't resolve) and `[2][3]`
is parsed as display text `2` with ref key `3` (so `[2]` won't resolve).

Examples:

# Todo
- Add new feature X [details](features.md#feature-x)
- Fix bug Y [1]

# Done
- Fixed issue Z [2],[3]

[1]: bugs.md#bug-y
[2]: issues.md#issue-z
[3]: fixes.md#fix-z

## Reference numbering

Every note file (`todo.md`, `chores-NN.md`, `done.md`) keeps a
file-local `# References` section at the bottom. Reference numbers
are scoped to that file — `[1]` in `chores-07.md` and `[1]` in
`chores-01.md` are independent slots that may point at completely
different URLs. New chores files start their numbering at `[1]`.

## Retiring Done entries

`todo.md`'s `## Done` section is a rolling buffer of recently shipped
work, not a permanent log. Move entries into `done.md` at two natural
beats:

- **Closing a ladder** — when the final `X.Y.Z` (no suffix) commit
  ships, decide which prior entries are no longer needed for nearby
  context and migrate them.
- **Opening a new ladder** — at `X.Y.Z-0`, do the same sweep before
  bumping the version.

Migration mechanics:

- Move the bullet itself from `todo.md > ## Done` to
  `done.md` (preserving the original ref number).
- Copy any references the moved entries cite into
  `done.md`'s `# References` section (those refs are file-local,
  so coexisting with `todo.md`'s namespace is fine).
- Prune any references in `todo.md > # References` no longer cited
  by anything in `## In Progress` / `## Todo` / `## Done`. This
  frees the numbers for future reuse.

1	# Chores-07.md
2	
3	General chores notes — design captures (forward-looking) and
4	post-implementation chore entries. Same shape as chores-01..06.md;
5	07 starts here only because 06 grew large during the 0.40.0 /
6	0.41.0 cycles and a new top-level cycle is a natural file
7	boundary.
8	
9	Subsection headers use the trailing-version format from CLAUDE.md
10	when they correspond to a release: `## Description (X.Y.Z)`.
11	Pre-implementation design captures may use a plain title; once
12	implemented, the title can become a release-versioned chore.
13	
14	## --scope enum refactor (0.42.0)
15	
16	Picks up the work the 0.41.0 cycle redirected away from. The
17	foundation captured in [2] (chores-06's `### 0.41.0-4: capture
18	--scope enum vocabulary`) lands here as code: the `Scope`
19	type becomes a sum (`Roles(Vec<Side>) | Single(PathBuf)`), the
20	flag surface unifies under `--scope` / `-s` with both keyword and
21	path forms, and every dual-repo-aware command migrates to the
22	new shape in dependency order.
23	
24	**Cycle steps (initial sketch — sub-step boundaries can shift
25	once each command's call sites are seen).**
26	
27	- **0.42.0-0** — this plan + version bump + new
28	  `notes/chores-07.md`. Notes only.
29	- **0.42.0-1** — `scope.rs` enum. Internal only — no
30	  CLI changes yet, no consumers updated. Existing
31	  `Scope(Vec<Side>)` → `enum Scope { Roles(Vec<Side>),
32	  Single(PathBuf) }`. Helpers (`has_code`, `has_bot`,
33	  `is_code_only`, `is_both`, `is_empty`) shift to operate
34	  on `Roles` only; `Single(_)` is a distinct mode. Tests
35	  follow.
36	- **0.42.0-2** — custom CLI value parser for `--scope` +
37	  retrofit `init`. Parser handles the keyword set
38	  (`code|bot|code,bot|bot,code`) and the prefixed-path
39	  form (`./...`, `/...`, `~/...`). Bare names that aren't
40	  keywords error with a "did you mean `./foo`?" hint.
41	  Init is the smallest consumer (it already takes
42	  `--scope=code|bot|code,bot`); migrating it first proves
43	  the parser end-to-end before sync's more complex
44	  resolution logic comes onto the new shape.
45	- **0.42.0-3** — retrofit `sync`. Drop `-R/--repo`. Add
46	  `-s` short form. Default-scope rules update to the
47	  three-state model from [2]: dual workspace →
48	  `Roles([Code, Bot])`, single-repo workspace →
49	  `Roles([Code])`, POR → `Single(cwd)`. `scope_to_repos`
50	  in `common.rs` updates to handle the `Single(_)`
51	  variant. Update sync's integration tests where they
52	  exercise the resolver chain.
53	- **0.42.0-4** — `push --scope`. State machine becomes
54	  scope-aware: each stage either runs or no-ops based on
55	  scope. `Single(<path>)` mode means single-repo push
56	  (no `commit-claude` / `bookmark-claude` /
57	  `finalize-claude`, no `ochid:` trailer). `Roles(...)`
58	  modes work as captured in chores-06 [1]. Persist scope
59	  in `PushState` so resumes use the same scope. Add
60	  integration tests for each scope shape.
61	- **0.42.0-5** — `finalize --scope`. Replace the existing
62	  `--repo` flag with `--scope` (`Roles` for the dual case,
63	  `Single(<path>)` for the single-repo case). `--repo`
64	  goes away; per the 0.41.0-3 capture this is the
65	  intentional break, not a deprecation.
66	- **0.42.0-6** — `clone --scope`. Parallel to init —
67	  bootstrap with the appropriate scope. Single-repo clone
68	  via the path form for `vc-template-x1`-shape remotes.
69	- **0.42.0-7** — Single(_) dogfood validation. Apply the
70	  full `sync → edit → push` flow against a fresh POR or
71	  single-repo fixture (likely the `vc-template-x1` repo
72	  itself, since it's the canonical single-repo target).
73	  Surface and fix anything the unit tests miss.
74	- **0.42.0 (final)** — cycle close-out. Drops the
75	  `-N` suffix; updates `notes/todo.md`'s In Progress and
76	  Done sections; chores-07 close-out subsection.
77	
78	**Deferred to later cycles (per [2]).**
79	
80	- `validate-desc` / `fix-desc` `--scope`. Read-side
81	  commands; `Single(_)` errors there (validate compares
82	  two repos by definition).
83	- `chid` / `desc` / `list` / `show` — CommonArgs sweep.
84	  All four pick up `--scope` via a shared change to
85	  `CommonArgs`; existing `-R/--repo` retires there too.
86	- `--message-file PATH` for push. Independent push
87	  feature; gates the CLAUDE.md refresh from CLAUDE2.
88	
89	**References.** [2] points back at chores-06's
90	`### 0.41.0-4: capture --scope enum vocabulary` for
91	the full vocabulary, type-model, and per-command
92	applicability matrix. Read that subsection first before
93	diving into any of the `-N` steps below.
94	
95	### 0.42.0-1: scope.rs enum
96	
97	Internal-only refactor: tuple-struct `Scope(Vec<Side>)` →
98	`enum Scope { Roles(Vec<Side>), Single(PathBuf) }`. No CLI
99	surface changes, no consumer logic changes — every existing
100	caller still constructs and consumes `Roles(_)`; `Single(_)`
101	is staged for the parser (-2) and the sync resolver (-3).
102	
103	- `src/scope.rs`: enum body + `PathBuf` import; helpers
104	  (`has_code` / `has_bot` / `is_*_only` / `is_both` /
105	  `is_empty`) reflect the `Roles` arm via `matches!`,
106	  returning `false` (resp. not-empty) on `Single(_)`. New
107	  test pins the `Single(_)` helper behavior; existing
108	  Roles tests retitled accordingly. `#[allow(dead_code)]`
109	  on the `Single` variant for the staging window.
110	- `src/common.rs`: `default_scope` returns
111	  `Scope::Roles(_)`; `scope_to_repos` matches on the enum
112	  and returns an explicit error for `Single(_)` carrying
113	  the `0.42.0-3` staging marker. New test locks that
114	  contract so the -3 wire-up is forced to update it.
115	- `src/sync.rs`: `Some(sides) => Scope::Roles(sides.clone())`
116	  in `resolve_args_to_repos`; integration-test
117	  constructors switch to `Scope::Roles(...)`.
118	- `src/init.rs`: `plan_init` constructs `Scope::Roles(...)`
119	  for both the default and explicit-flag paths. Helper
120	  call sites (`is_code_only`, `is_both`, `is_bot_only`)
121	  unchanged — the methods continue to work on `Roles`.
122	
123	Build is warning-clean; 257 tests pass (255 baseline + 2
124	new in scope/common).
125	
126	### 0.42.0-2: --scope parser + retrofit init
127	
128	Lands the custom CLI value parser and migrates `init` onto
129	the new `Scope` shape. Parser is the shared boundary every
130	remaining `--scope` consumer (sync, push, finalize, clone)
131	will route through in -3+; init is the smallest first
132	consumer and exercises the keyword path end-to-end. The
133	path form is accepted by the parser but rejected at init
134	with a hint pointing at `--repo-local` / `--repo-remote`
135	(init creates a workspace, not a single-repo project).
136	
137	- `src/scope.rs`: `pub fn parse_scope(s: &str) -> Result<Scope, String>`.
138	  Accepts the four keyword forms (`code`, `bot`, `code,bot`,
139	  `bot,code`) plus path forms prefixed by `./`, `../`, `/`,
140	  `~/`, or the bare `~`. Anything else errors with a hint
141	  pointing at the prefix-disambiguation rule. Order is
142	  preserved on the keyword forms. Drops the now-dead
143	  `is_empty` helper (parser rejects empty input upstream;
144	  the only consumer that called it was init's redundant
145	  validation). 13 new parser unit tests.
146	- `src/init.rs`: `--scope` field flips from
147	  `Option<Vec<Side>>` (clap `value_delimiter = ','`) to
148	  `Option<Scope>` with `value_parser = crate::scope::parse_scope`.
149	  `plan_init` matches on the parsed `Scope`: `Roles(_)`
150	  takes the existing bot-only-fatal path; `Single(_)` is
151	  rejected with the init-specific hint. Test scaffolding
152	  (`fixture_scoped`) and 5 fixture call sites migrated.
153	  `scope_parses_*` tests retitled around the new field
154	  type. New `scope_path_form_rejected_at_init` test pins
155	  the rejection contract.
156	
157	Smoke-tested end-to-end via CLI:
158	
159	- `vc-x1 init tf1 --scope code --dry-run` → succeeds
160	  (single-repo dry-run plan).
161	- `vc-x1 init tf1 --scope code,bot --dry-run` → succeeds.
162	- `vc-x1 init tf1 --scope bot --dry-run` → bot-only fatal
163	  (existing `plan_init` check).
164	- `vc-x1 init tf1 --scope ./foo --dry-run` → path-form
165	  fatal (new `plan_init` check).
166	- `vc-x1 init tf1 --scope '~/work' --dry-run` → path-form
167	  fatal.
168	- `vc-x1 init tf1 --scope foo --dry-run` → clap-level
169	  parser error with hint about `./foo` disambiguation.
170	
171	### 0.42.0-3: sync --scope retrofit
172	
173	Migrates `sync` onto the new `Scope` shape and lands the
174	`Single(_)` resolver wiring. `-R/--repo` retires (single-
175	repo callers move to `--scope=<path>`); `-s` becomes the
176	short form of `--scope`. Default-scope rules pick up the
177	three-state model captured in chores-06's [1] (-4
178	vocabulary): dual workspace → `Roles([Code, Bot])`,
179	single-repo workspace → `Roles([Code])`, POR →
180	`Single(cwd)`.
181	
182	- `src/common.rs`: `default_scope` signature gains a `cwd`
183	  parameter; the POR branch (no workspace_root) now
184	  returns `Scope::Single(cwd.to_path_buf())` instead of
185	  `Roles([Code])`. `scope_to_repos`'s `Single(p)` arm
186	  resolves to `vec![p.clone()]` (no workspace lookup;
187	  shell-style expansion happens at the parser/consumer
188	  boundary). The `0.42.0-3` staging-error test is
189	  replaced with `scope_to_repos_single_returns_path` —
190	  the happy-path contract — and `default_scope_por`
191	  becomes `default_scope_por_returns_single_cwd`.
192	- `src/sync.rs`: `--repo`/`-R` flag and the `repos:
193	  Vec<PathBuf>` field deleted. `--scope` retypes from
194	  `Option<Vec<Side>>` (with `value_delimiter = ','`) to
195	  `Option<Scope>` (with `value_parser = parse_scope`)
196	  and gains `short = 's'`. SyncArgs doc rewritten around
197	  the new resolution rules. `resolve_args_to_repos`
198	  drops the `args.repos` branch and now feeds `cwd` into
199	  `default_scope`. `split_repos` and its 4 unit tests
200	  go away with `-R`; `parse_scope_repo_conflict`,
201	  `parse_single_repo_flag`, `parse_repeated_repo_flag`
202	  too. `parse_scope_*` tests assert against `Some(Scope)`;
203	  new `parse_scope_path_form` and `parse_scope_short_form`
204	  tests pin the new entry points. `apply_args` integration
205	  helper drops the `repos` field. `Side` import is gated
206	  `#[cfg(test)]` (production sync no longer needs it
207	  directly — only the tests construct `Side::*`).
208	
209	Smoke-tested end-to-end:
210	
211	- `vc-x1 sync --check` in this dual workspace → `2 repos,
212	  all bookmarks up-to-date`.
213	- `vc-x1 sync --check -s code` → `1 repo, all bookmarks
214	  up-to-date`.
215	- `vc-x1 sync --check -s bot` → ditto for `.claude`.
216	- `vc-x1 sync --check -s ./.claude` → also `1 repo` —
217	  Single-mode resolves directly to that path, bypassing
218	  the workspace's other-repo lookup.
219	- `cd /tmp && vc-x1 sync --check` → POR detected,
220	  attempts to sync `/tmp` (errors because `/tmp` isn't a
221	  jj repo — confirms `default_scope` returned
222	  `Single(cwd)`).
223	
224	## init + clone redesign (0.41.1)
225	
226	Empirical validation 2026-04-27 against `vc-x1 0.42.0-3` (see
227	`notes/vc-x1-init.md`) surfaced cosmetic anomalies and a
228	substantive design gap. Init's flag surface (`--repo-local`,
229	`--repo-remote`, `--owner`, `--dir`, `[NAME]`) carries 6+
230	mutually-exclusive preflight checks just to prevent impossible
231	combinations from being typed. Clone already implements a
232	unified-positional alternative (URL / `owner/name` shorthand);
233	this cycle extends that pattern to init, adds POR support to
234	both, and unifies their CLI surfaces.
235	
236	Branched off 0.41.0 (commit `6747a27`). On close-out, the
237	in-flight 0.42.0 work rebases on top.
238	
239	### Command structure
240	
241	```
242	vc-x1 init  <TARGET> [NAME] [--scope code,bot|por] [--private] [--dry-run]
243	vc-x1 clone <TARGET> [NAME] [--scope code,bot|por]             [--dry-run]
244	```
245	
246	Identical surfaces modulo `--private` (init only).
247	
248	`<TARGET>` accepts:
249	
250	- **URL** — `git@host:owner/name(.git)?`, `https://...(.git)?`
251	  (detected by `://` or SSH `<host>:<path>` shape with `@`).
252	- **`owner/name` shorthand** — single `/`, no path prefix;
253	  resolved to `git@github.com:owner/name.git`.
254	- **Path-prefixed** — `./X`, `../X`, `/X`, `~/X`, `~`. Path
255	  IS the target; last component is the workspace name. Bare
256	  `.` and bare `NAME` are errors (require explicit prefix).
257	
258	`[NAME]`:
259	
260	- For URL / `owner/name` forms: overrides the derived
261	  destination dir name. `vc-x1 clone owner/foo my-name`
262	  clones into `./my-name/` instead of `./foo/`.
263	- For path-prefix form: error if combined (path already
264	  specifies the full target).
265	- Path-form for clone: error (clone needs a remote URL;
266	  see "Operations" for clone's target rules).
267	
268	`--scope`:
269	
270	- `code,bot` (default): both repos, dual-repo layout.
271	- `por`: single repo, no `.vc-config.toml` written.
272	- `code` / `bot` standalone: error. Reason — these are
273	  config-lookup keywords; init has no config to look up
274	  against, and clone's simplification omits them too. The
275	  manual decomposition (clone code as POR, clone bot as
276	  POR, place at `.claude/`, run `vc-x1 symlink`) covers
277	  the rare cases where a user wants the dual layout
278	  composed by hand.
279	
280	`--private`: only meaningful for init when creating a new
281	GitHub repo. Sets visibility at `gh repo create`. Silently
282	warn-and-ignore if the remote already exists. Errors on
283	clone (clone never creates a remote).
284	
285	`--dry-run`: validate the operation as far as possible
286	without side effects. Print what would be done.
287	
288	### Operations
289	
290	**Init** is *smart* — it detects the target dir's state and
291	chooses the right operation.
292	
293	| Target state | `--scope=code,bot` | `--scope=por` |
294	|---|---|---|
295	| empty / nonexistent | create dual-repo from scratch | create POR |
296	| POR (jj+git, no config) | upgrade to dual | error (already POR) |
297	| POR (git only, no `.jj/`) | bootstrap `jj git init`, then upgrade | error (already POR-shape) |
298	| code POR + `.claude/` peer POR (configs missing) | write configs only | error |
299	| dual-repo (configs present) | error (already there) | error (downgrade) |
300	| single-repo (illegal shape) | error | error |
301	| anything else (random files, etc.) | error with diagnostic | error with diagnostic |
302	
303	**Clone** is *dumb* — it just clones URLs into target dirs.
304	
305	| `--scope=por` | clone the URL into `<target>` |
306	| `--scope=code,bot` | clone code URL into `<target>`, derive bot URL (`<URL>.claude`), clone into `<target>/.claude/`, then create symlink via `vc-x1 symlink` |
307	
308	For both clone modes: target must NOT exist (`vc-x1 clone`
309	errors if anything is there). Use `vc-x1 sync` to update an
310	existing checkout.
311	
312	`.vc-config.toml` files for the dual-repo case are *tracked
313	content in the remotes*, not files clone writes. They ride
314	along with the clone. If a remote doesn't have them
315	committed, `vc-x1 init <path> --scope=code,bot` from inside
316	the cloned dir handles the config-only upgrade case (see
317	init's state table above).
318	
319	`--scope=code,bot` is implemented as in-process composition
320	of the `--scope=por` primitive (called twice: once for code
321	URL, once for derived bot URL) plus a `symlink::create`
322	call. No subprocess overhead.
323	
324	If init or clone fails partway through, manual cleanup is
325	required (no rollback).
326	
327	### Example Layout local repos
328	
329	cwd = ~/prgs
330	
331	`vc-init test-repos/tf1 --scope=code,bot`
332	`vc-init test-repos/tf1`
333	
334	TODO: add actual output of ls or tree cmds (fill at close-out)
335	
336	`vc-init ../test-repos/tf1 --scope=code,bot`
337	`vc-init ../test-repos/tf1`
338	
339	TODO: add actual output of ls or tree cmds (fill at close-out)
340	
341	### Preflight
342	
343	- Verify `jj` is installed (`jj --version`); friendly error
344	  with install link if missing.
345	- For init URL / `owner/name` forms: probe with `git
346	  ls-remote <url>`. Exists → clone. Doesn't → create via
347	  `gh repo create` (errors if host isn't GitHub or `gh`
348	  missing).
349	- For clone URL / `owner/name` forms: probe with `git
350	  ls-remote <url>`. Exists → clone. Doesn't → error
351	  (clone is dumb, no auto-create).
352	
353	### Edits
354	
355	- `Cargo.toml`: bump to `0.41.1`.
356	- New `src/repo_url.rs` (or fold into `src/common.rs`): lift
357	  `derive_name` and `resolve_url` from `clone.rs`. Add
358	  `parse_target(s) -> Target` enum `{Url(String),
359	  OwnerName(String, String), Path(PathBuf)}`. Single source
360	  of truth for positional parsing across init and clone.
361	- `src/clone.rs`:
362	  - `CloneArgs`: `<TARGET>` + optional `[NAME]` positionals;
363	    add `--scope code,bot|por` (default `code,bot`); drop
364	    `--dir`. Old `--repo` positional rename: `repo` →
365	    `target`.
366	  - Refactor body into `clone_one(url, target_dir)` (the
367	    primitive, also used by `--scope=por`) and
368	    `clone_dual(code_url, target_dir)` (orchestrator —
369	    derives bot URL, calls `clone_one` twice, calls
370	    `symlink::create`).
371	  - Pre-clone check: error if `target_dir` exists.
372	- `src/init.rs`:
373	  - `InitArgs`: drop `owner`, `repo_local`, `repo_remote`,
374	    `dir`; replace bare-name `name` positional with
375	    `<TARGET>` + optional `[NAME]`.
376	  - Remove the 6 mutually-exclusive flag checks.
377	  - New `detect_state(path) -> InitTargetState`
378	    `{Empty, PorJjGit, PorGitOnly, PorWithPeerPor,
379	    SingleRepoWorkspace, DualRepoWorkspace, Other(reason)}`.
380	  - Refactor body into `init_one(target_dir, opts)` (the
381	    `--scope=por` primitive, also used as a step inside
382	    `--scope=code,bot`) and `init_dual(target_dir, opts)`
383	    (orchestrator — calls `init_one` twice, writes both
384	    `.vc-config.toml` files, ochid-links, `symlink::create`).
385	  - POR upgrade paths:
386	    - PorJjGit + `--scope=code,bot` → skip code-side init,
387	      run bot-side `init_one` into `.claude/`, write configs,
388	      ochid-link existing code commit + new bot commit,
389	      symlink.
390	    - PorGitOnly + `--scope=code,bot` → `jj git init` first,
391	      then PorJjGit path.
392	    - PorWithPeerPor + `--scope=code,bot` → write only the
393	      two `.vc-config.toml` files + symlink (both repos
394	      already exist).
395	- `src/test_helpers.rs`: `Fixture::new_opts` reshapes to use
396	  the new `target` positional shape (and `[NAME]` if needed).
397	- `notes/todo.md`: add this cycle to In Progress.
398	- `notes/chores-07.md`: per-step post-impl subsections.
399	- `notes/vc-x1-init.md`: close out cosmetic anomalies (fold
400	  into final close-out).
401	
402	### Cycle structure — multi-step
403	
404	- `-0` — plan + version bump + this notes section.
405	- `-1` — lift `derive_name` / `resolve_url` / `parse_target`
406	  to shared module; clone migrates internally (no behavior
407	  change yet).
408	- `-2` — clone reshape: `<TARGET>` + `[NAME]` positionals,
409	  add `--scope code,bot|por`, refactor into `clone_one` /
410	  `clone_dual`. Add target-exists pre-check.
411	- `-3` — init reshape: drop old flags, add `<TARGET>` +
412	  `[NAME]`, add `--scope=por`, refactor into `init_one` /
413	  `init_dual`. Existing create-from-empty operations work
414	  via the new shape.
415	- `-4` — init POR detection + upgrade paths
416	  (PorJjGit, PorGitOnly auto-bootstrap, PorWithPeerPor
417	  config-only).
418	- `-5` — `test_helpers::Fixture` migration; audit downstream
419	  callers across the test suite.
420	- final — cycle close-out: fill in Example Layout outputs,
421	  address `notes/vc-x1-init.md` cosmetic anomalies, drop
422	  the `-N` suffix.
423	
424	### Decisions made during design
425	
426	- **Version + cycle line.** This work + the sync `--check`
427	  fix land on the 0.41.x line. Init+clone = 0.41.1. Sync
428	  fix = a separate cycle (likely 0.41.2). Then rebase the
429	  in-flight 0.42.0 work on top of both.
430	- **Path-prefix vocabulary.** `./NAME` and the standard
431	  prefixes (`../`, `/`, `~/`, `~`) only. Bare `.` and bare
432	  `NAME` are errors — explicit prefix required.
433	- **`--private` on existing remote.** Warn and ignore;
434	  visibility was set at create time.
435	- **Cosmetic anomalies** from `notes/vc-x1-init.md` —
436	  addressed at close-out, not deferred.
437	- **`--scope=code` and `--scope=bot` for clone.** Dropped
438	  from the menu. Manual decomposition (two `--scope=por`
439	  clones + `vc-x1 symlink`) covers the use case.
440	- **Composition over duplication.** `--scope=code,bot` is
441	  implemented as in-process composition of the `--scope=por`
442	  primitive — single source of truth for the actual
443	  clone/init operation, thin wrapper for the dual case.
444	
445	# References
446	
447	[1]: /notes/chores-06.md#--scope-continuation-0410
448	[2]: /notes/chores-06.md#0410-4-capture---scope-enum-vocabulary
449

# Chores-07.md

General chores notes — design captures (forward-looking) and
post-implementation chore entries. Same shape as chores-01..06.md;
07 starts here only because 06 grew large during the 0.40.0 /
0.41.0 cycles and a new top-level cycle is a natural file
boundary.

Subsection headers use the trailing-version format from CLAUDE.md
when they correspond to a release: `## Description (X.Y.Z)`.
Pre-implementation design captures may use a plain title; once
implemented, the title can become a release-versioned chore.

## --scope enum refactor (0.42.0)

Picks up the work the 0.41.0 cycle redirected away from. The
foundation captured in [2] (chores-06's `### 0.41.0-4: capture
--scope enum vocabulary`) lands here as code: the `Scope`
type becomes a sum (`Roles(Vec<Side>) | Single(PathBuf)`), the
flag surface unifies under `--scope` / `-s` with both keyword and
path forms, and every dual-repo-aware command migrates to the
new shape in dependency order.

**Cycle steps (initial sketch — sub-step boundaries can shift
once each command's call sites are seen).**

- **0.42.0-0** — this plan + version bump + new
  `notes/chores-07.md`. Notes only.
- **0.42.0-1** — `scope.rs` enum. Internal only — no
  CLI changes yet, no consumers updated. Existing
  `Scope(Vec<Side>)` → `enum Scope { Roles(Vec<Side>),
  Single(PathBuf) }`. Helpers (`has_code`, `has_bot`,
  `is_code_only`, `is_both`, `is_empty`) shift to operate
  on `Roles` only; `Single(_)` is a distinct mode. Tests
  follow.
- **0.42.0-2** — custom CLI value parser for `--scope` +
  retrofit `init`. Parser handles the keyword set
  (`code|bot|code,bot|bot,code`) and the prefixed-path
  form (`./...`, `/...`, `~/...`). Bare names that aren't
  keywords error with a "did you mean `./foo`?" hint.
  Init is the smallest consumer (it already takes
  `--scope=code|bot|code,bot`); migrating it first proves
  the parser end-to-end before sync's more complex
  resolution logic comes onto the new shape.
- **0.42.0-3** — retrofit `sync`. Drop `-R/--repo`. Add
  `-s` short form. Default-scope rules update to the
  three-state model from [2]: dual workspace →
  `Roles([Code, Bot])`, single-repo workspace →
  `Roles([Code])`, POR → `Single(cwd)`. `scope_to_repos`
  in `common.rs` updates to handle the `Single(_)`
  variant. Update sync's integration tests where they
  exercise the resolver chain.
- **0.42.0-4** — `push --scope`. State machine becomes
  scope-aware: each stage either runs or no-ops based on
  scope. `Single(<path>)` mode means single-repo push
  (no `commit-claude` / `bookmark-claude` /
  `finalize-claude`, no `ochid:` trailer). `Roles(...)`
  modes work as captured in chores-06 [1]. Persist scope
  in `PushState` so resumes use the same scope. Add
  integration tests for each scope shape.
- **0.42.0-5** — `finalize --scope`. Replace the existing
  `--repo` flag with `--scope` (`Roles` for the dual case,
  `Single(<path>)` for the single-repo case). `--repo`
  goes away; per the 0.41.0-3 capture this is the
  intentional break, not a deprecation.
- **0.42.0-6** — `clone --scope`. Parallel to init —
  bootstrap with the appropriate scope. Single-repo clone
  via the path form for `vc-template-x1`-shape remotes.
- **0.42.0-7** — Single(_) dogfood validation. Apply the
  full `sync → edit → push` flow against a fresh POR or
  single-repo fixture (likely the `vc-template-x1` repo
  itself, since it's the canonical single-repo target).
  Surface and fix anything the unit tests miss.
- **0.42.0 (final)** — cycle close-out. Drops the
  `-N` suffix; updates `notes/todo.md`'s In Progress and
  Done sections; chores-07 close-out subsection.

**Deferred to later cycles (per [2]).**

- `validate-desc` / `fix-desc` `--scope`. Read-side
  commands; `Single(_)` errors there (validate compares
  two repos by definition).
- `chid` / `desc` / `list` / `show` — CommonArgs sweep.
  All four pick up `--scope` via a shared change to
  `CommonArgs`; existing `-R/--repo` retires there too.
- `--message-file PATH` for push. Independent push
  feature; gates the CLAUDE.md refresh from CLAUDE2.

**References.** [2] points back at chores-06's
`### 0.41.0-4: capture --scope enum vocabulary` for
the full vocabulary, type-model, and per-command
applicability matrix. Read that subsection first before
diving into any of the `-N` steps below.

### 0.42.0-1: scope.rs enum

Internal-only refactor: tuple-struct `Scope(Vec<Side>)` →
`enum Scope { Roles(Vec<Side>), Single(PathBuf) }`. No CLI
surface changes, no consumer logic changes — every existing
caller still constructs and consumes `Roles(_)`; `Single(_)`
is staged for the parser (-2) and the sync resolver (-3).

- `src/scope.rs`: enum body + `PathBuf` import; helpers
  (`has_code` / `has_bot` / `is_*_only` / `is_both` /
  `is_empty`) reflect the `Roles` arm via `matches!`,
  returning `false` (resp. not-empty) on `Single(_)`. New
  test pins the `Single(_)` helper behavior; existing
  Roles tests retitled accordingly. `#[allow(dead_code)]`
  on the `Single` variant for the staging window.
- `src/common.rs`: `default_scope` returns
  `Scope::Roles(_)`; `scope_to_repos` matches on the enum
  and returns an explicit error for `Single(_)` carrying
  the `0.42.0-3` staging marker. New test locks that
  contract so the -3 wire-up is forced to update it.
- `src/sync.rs`: `Some(sides) => Scope::Roles(sides.clone())`
  in `resolve_args_to_repos`; integration-test
  constructors switch to `Scope::Roles(...)`.
- `src/init.rs`: `plan_init` constructs `Scope::Roles(...)`
  for both the default and explicit-flag paths. Helper
  call sites (`is_code_only`, `is_both`, `is_bot_only`)
  unchanged — the methods continue to work on `Roles`.

Build is warning-clean; 257 tests pass (255 baseline + 2
new in scope/common).

### 0.42.0-2: --scope parser + retrofit init

Lands the custom CLI value parser and migrates `init` onto
the new `Scope` shape. Parser is the shared boundary every
remaining `--scope` consumer (sync, push, finalize, clone)
will route through in -3+; init is the smallest first
consumer and exercises the keyword path end-to-end. The
path form is accepted by the parser but rejected at init
with a hint pointing at `--repo-local` / `--repo-remote`
(init creates a workspace, not a single-repo project).

- `src/scope.rs`: `pub fn parse_scope(s: &str) -> Result<Scope, String>`.
  Accepts the four keyword forms (`code`, `bot`, `code,bot`,
  `bot,code`) plus path forms prefixed by `./`, `../`, `/`,
  `~/`, or the bare `~`. Anything else errors with a hint
  pointing at the prefix-disambiguation rule. Order is
  preserved on the keyword forms. Drops the now-dead
  `is_empty` helper (parser rejects empty input upstream;
  the only consumer that called it was init's redundant
  validation). 13 new parser unit tests.
- `src/init.rs`: `--scope` field flips from
  `Option<Vec<Side>>` (clap `value_delimiter = ','`) to
  `Option<Scope>` with `value_parser = crate::scope::parse_scope`.
  `plan_init` matches on the parsed `Scope`: `Roles(_)`
  takes the existing bot-only-fatal path; `Single(_)` is
  rejected with the init-specific hint. Test scaffolding
  (`fixture_scoped`) and 5 fixture call sites migrated.
  `scope_parses_*` tests retitled around the new field
  type. New `scope_path_form_rejected_at_init` test pins
  the rejection contract.

Smoke-tested end-to-end via CLI:

- `vc-x1 init tf1 --scope code --dry-run` → succeeds
  (single-repo dry-run plan).
- `vc-x1 init tf1 --scope code,bot --dry-run` → succeeds.
- `vc-x1 init tf1 --scope bot --dry-run` → bot-only fatal
  (existing `plan_init` check).
- `vc-x1 init tf1 --scope ./foo --dry-run` → path-form
  fatal (new `plan_init` check).
- `vc-x1 init tf1 --scope '~/work' --dry-run` → path-form
  fatal.
- `vc-x1 init tf1 --scope foo --dry-run` → clap-level
  parser error with hint about `./foo` disambiguation.

### 0.42.0-3: sync --scope retrofit

Migrates `sync` onto the new `Scope` shape and lands the
`Single(_)` resolver wiring. `-R/--repo` retires (single-
repo callers move to `--scope=<path>`); `-s` becomes the
short form of `--scope`. Default-scope rules pick up the
three-state model captured in chores-06's [1] (-4
vocabulary): dual workspace → `Roles([Code, Bot])`,
single-repo workspace → `Roles([Code])`, POR →
`Single(cwd)`.

- `src/common.rs`: `default_scope` signature gains a `cwd`
  parameter; the POR branch (no workspace_root) now
  returns `Scope::Single(cwd.to_path_buf())` instead of
  `Roles([Code])`. `scope_to_repos`'s `Single(p)` arm
  resolves to `vec![p.clone()]` (no workspace lookup;
  shell-style expansion happens at the parser/consumer
  boundary). The `0.42.0-3` staging-error test is
  replaced with `scope_to_repos_single_returns_path` —
  the happy-path contract — and `default_scope_por`
  becomes `default_scope_por_returns_single_cwd`.
- `src/sync.rs`: `--repo`/`-R` flag and the `repos:
  Vec<PathBuf>` field deleted. `--scope` retypes from
  `Option<Vec<Side>>` (with `value_delimiter = ','`) to
  `Option<Scope>` (with `value_parser = parse_scope`)
  and gains `short = 's'`. SyncArgs doc rewritten around
  the new resolution rules. `resolve_args_to_repos`
  drops the `args.repos` branch and now feeds `cwd` into
  `default_scope`. `split_repos` and its 4 unit tests
  go away with `-R`; `parse_scope_repo_conflict`,
  `parse_single_repo_flag`, `parse_repeated_repo_flag`
  too. `parse_scope_*` tests assert against `Some(Scope)`;
  new `parse_scope_path_form` and `parse_scope_short_form`
  tests pin the new entry points. `apply_args` integration
  helper drops the `repos` field. `Side` import is gated
  `#[cfg(test)]` (production sync no longer needs it
  directly — only the tests construct `Side::*`).

Smoke-tested end-to-end:

- `vc-x1 sync --check` in this dual workspace → `2 repos,
  all bookmarks up-to-date`.
- `vc-x1 sync --check -s code` → `1 repo, all bookmarks
  up-to-date`.
- `vc-x1 sync --check -s bot` → ditto for `.claude`.
- `vc-x1 sync --check -s ./.claude` → also `1 repo` —
  Single-mode resolves directly to that path, bypassing
  the workspace's other-repo lookup.
- `cd /tmp && vc-x1 sync --check` → POR detected,
  attempts to sync `/tmp` (errors because `/tmp` isn't a
  jj repo — confirms `default_scope` returned
  `Single(cwd)`).

## init + clone redesign (0.41.1)

Empirical validation 2026-04-27 against `vc-x1 0.42.0-3` (see
`notes/vc-x1-init.md`) surfaced cosmetic anomalies and a
substantive design gap. Init's flag surface (`--repo-local`,
`--repo-remote`, `--owner`, `--dir`, `[NAME]`) carries 6+
mutually-exclusive preflight checks just to prevent impossible
combinations from being typed. Clone already implements a
unified-positional alternative (URL / `owner/name` shorthand);
this cycle extends that pattern to init, adds POR support to
both, and unifies their CLI surfaces.

Branched off 0.41.0 (commit `6747a27`). On close-out, the
in-flight 0.42.0 work rebases on top.

### Command structure

```
vc-x1 init  <TARGET> [NAME] [--scope code,bot|por] [--private] [--dry-run]
vc-x1 clone <TARGET> [NAME] [--scope code,bot|por]             [--dry-run]
```

Identical surfaces modulo `--private` (init only).

`<TARGET>` accepts:

- **URL** — `git@host:owner/name(.git)?`, `https://...(.git)?`
  (detected by `://` or SSH `<host>:<path>` shape with `@`).
- **`owner/name` shorthand** — single `/`, no path prefix;
  resolved to `git@github.com:owner/name.git`.
- **Path-prefixed** — `./X`, `../X`, `/X`, `~/X`, `~`. Path
  IS the target; last component is the workspace name. Bare
  `.` and bare `NAME` are errors (require explicit prefix).

`[NAME]`:

- For URL / `owner/name` forms: overrides the derived
  destination dir name. `vc-x1 clone owner/foo my-name`
  clones into `./my-name/` instead of `./foo/`.
- For path-prefix form: error if combined (path already
  specifies the full target).
- Path-form for clone: error (clone needs a remote URL;
  see "Operations" for clone's target rules).

`--scope`:

- `code,bot` (default): both repos, dual-repo layout.
- `por`: single repo, no `.vc-config.toml` written.
- `code` / `bot` standalone: error. Reason — these are
  config-lookup keywords; init has no config to look up
  against, and clone's simplification omits them too. The
  manual decomposition (clone code as POR, clone bot as
  POR, place at `.claude/`, run `vc-x1 symlink`) covers
  the rare cases where a user wants the dual layout
  composed by hand.

`--private`: only meaningful for init when creating a new
GitHub repo. Sets visibility at `gh repo create`. Silently
warn-and-ignore if the remote already exists. Errors on
clone (clone never creates a remote).

`--dry-run`: validate the operation as far as possible
without side effects. Print what would be done.

### Operations

**Init** is *smart* — it detects the target dir's state and
chooses the right operation.

| Target state | `--scope=code,bot` | `--scope=por` |
|---|---|---|
| empty / nonexistent | create dual-repo from scratch | create POR |
| POR (jj+git, no config) | upgrade to dual | error (already POR) |
| POR (git only, no `.jj/`) | bootstrap `jj git init`, then upgrade | error (already POR-shape) |
| code POR + `.claude/` peer POR (configs missing) | write configs only | error |
| dual-repo (configs present) | error (already there) | error (downgrade) |
| single-repo (illegal shape) | error | error |
| anything else (random files, etc.) | error with diagnostic | error with diagnostic |

**Clone** is *dumb* — it just clones URLs into target dirs.

| `--scope=por` | clone the URL into `<target>` |
| `--scope=code,bot` | clone code URL into `<target>`, derive bot URL (`<URL>.claude`), clone into `<target>/.claude/`, then create symlink via `vc-x1 symlink` |

For both clone modes: target must NOT exist (`vc-x1 clone`
errors if anything is there). Use `vc-x1 sync` to update an
existing checkout.

`.vc-config.toml` files for the dual-repo case are *tracked
content in the remotes*, not files clone writes. They ride
along with the clone. If a remote doesn't have them
committed, `vc-x1 init <path> --scope=code,bot` from inside
the cloned dir handles the config-only upgrade case (see
init's state table above).

`--scope=code,bot` is implemented as in-process composition
of the `--scope=por` primitive (called twice: once for code
URL, once for derived bot URL) plus a `symlink::create`
call. No subprocess overhead.

If init or clone fails partway through, manual cleanup is
required (no rollback).

### Example Layout local repos

cwd = ~/prgs

`vc-init test-repos/tf1 --scope=code,bot`
`vc-init test-repos/tf1`

TODO: add actual output of ls or tree cmds (fill at close-out)

`vc-init ../test-repos/tf1 --scope=code,bot`
`vc-init ../test-repos/tf1`

TODO: add actual output of ls or tree cmds (fill at close-out)

### Preflight

- Verify `jj` is installed (`jj --version`); friendly error
  with install link if missing.
- For init URL / `owner/name` forms: probe with `git
  ls-remote <url>`. Exists → clone. Doesn't → create via
  `gh repo create` (errors if host isn't GitHub or `gh`
  missing).
- For clone URL / `owner/name` forms: probe with `git
  ls-remote <url>`. Exists → clone. Doesn't → error
  (clone is dumb, no auto-create).

### Edits

- `Cargo.toml`: bump to `0.41.1`.
- New `src/repo_url.rs` (or fold into `src/common.rs`): lift
  `derive_name` and `resolve_url` from `clone.rs`. Add
  `parse_target(s) -> Target` enum `{Url(String),
  OwnerName(String, String), Path(PathBuf)}`. Single source
  of truth for positional parsing across init and clone.
- `src/clone.rs`:
  - `CloneArgs`: `<TARGET>` + optional `[NAME]` positionals;
    add `--scope code,bot|por` (default `code,bot`); drop
    `--dir`. Old `--repo` positional rename: `repo` →
    `target`.

content.text:
Thoughts on the fork:

## chid stability — you're fine

jj's two-id system is built for this. `commit_id` (the git SHA) changes on rewrite; `change_id` is stable through rebase, squash-into-self, describe — anything that doesn't *fold* commits. ochid trailers point at change_ids. So when 0.42.0-0..-4 later rebases on top of 0.41.1, the chids are preserved and every ochid trailer (in both directions) still resolves. The fork doesn't break the cross-ref scheme.

The risk is `jj squash` / `jj abandon` — those collapse or remove change_ids. If we just rebase and describe, we're safe.

## time isn't a better primitive

Two problems with time-based linking: clock skew between repos (commits are authored in different processes/sessions), and time doesn't solve the rewrite problem any better than chid does — you still need a stable identifier baked into the trailer. If we ever distrust chid, the answer is jj hygiene (don't squash-collapse during rebase), not switching primitives. I'd keep chid.

## fork mechanics

Current state: `55eadc8` (0.42.0-4) is just the 0.41.1 docs capture — Cargo.toml bump + chores-07's 0.41.1 section + `notes/vc-x1-init.md`. The "push --scope" work that 0.42.0-4 was nominally for hasn't started.

Cleanest move:

1. **New bookmark** `0.41.1-init-clone` at `6747a27` (the 0.41.0 close-out).
2. **Rebase 55eadc8 onto 6747a27** — `jj rebase -r 55eadc8 -d 6747a27`. This preserves its chid, so the bot's ochid trailer still resolves; the docs land on the new branch as 0.41.1-0.
3. **Rewrite the description + version** in the rebased commit: `(0.42.0-4)` → `(0.41.1-0)`, Cargo.toml `0.42.0-4` → `0.41.1-0`. Probably also relocate the chores section out of `chores-07.md` (which is the 0.42.0-cycle file) into its own file (`chores-08.md` or similar) so the future 0.42.0 rebase doesn't collide on chores-07.
4. **Move `main` back** to `4d8709a` (0.42.0-3, the previous 0.42.0 commit). Reset the bookmark; force-push.
5. **Push the new bookmark.**
6. **Clear `.vc-x1/push-state.toml`** — any saved state from the prior push is stale after the rewind.

Bot repo: untouched. The bot commit that paired with 55eadc8 keeps its ochid trailer; it just now points at a chid living on a different branch instead of main. That's correct — it reflects the actual history of *this* session, which is linear.

## frictions worth flagging

- **Force-push to main.** Destructive, needs your explicit go-ahead. Anyone with `55eadc8` fetched will need to reset.
- **Chores file split.** `chores-07.md` opened for 0.42.0 — the 0.41.1 section bolted onto it should probably move to its own file on the new branch to avoid rebase conflicts later.
- **Cross-ref check after the move.** Run `vc-x1 chid -R .,.claude -L` (or the validate flow) on the rebased commit to confirm the bot side's ochid trailer still resolves to it.
- **Validation that chids actually survive** the specific rebase we're doing — the theory is solid but worth empirically checking the first time we do this. If they don't, that's a real gap and *then* we'd talk about alternatives.

Do you want me to proceed with this plan, or do you want to push back on any of it first?

content.text:
Got it — main as recovery anchor, init-clone-refactor as a side branch, rebase later. That's safer.

## Revised plan

1. **Create `init-clone-refactor` bookmark at `6747a27`** (code side).
2. **Re-author the 0.41.1-0 commit fresh** on that branch — don't try to cherry-pick from `55eadc8`. The content has the wrong version (0.42.0-4), wrong file home (chores-07 is the 0.42.0-cycle file), and a bot-side ochid trailer pointing at the wrong session. Easier to write the right commit than to mutate the wrong one.
3. **`55eadc8` stays put on main** as the recovery anchor. It becomes redundant once init-clone-refactor lands and gets dropped during the eventual main rebase.
4. **Freeze main** for the duration of init-clone-refactor — no new 0.42.0 commits while the side branch is active. Otherwise we'd be deciding on the rebase strategy under deadline pressure later.
5. **Chores file:** new `notes/chores-08.md` for the 0.41.1 cycle, instead of folding into chores-07. Keeps the 0.42.0 chores file untouched on main, so the eventual rebase has zero overlap on chores.
6. **At close-out:** rebase main onto init-clone-refactor; drop `55eadc8` in the rebase; verify ochid trailers resolve via `vc-x1 chid -R .,.claude -L` on the rebased commits.

## Bot-side question to settle now

`.claude` is linear and the current session has already advanced past `6747a27`'s pair. When we push the code-side `init-clone-refactor` branch, what does the bot side do? `vc-x1 push <bookmark>` assumes symmetric bookmark names. Three options:

- **(a) Mirror bookmark** — create `init-clone-refactor` on `.claude` *at current `.claude` main HEAD* (not at the historical 0.41.0-paired position). New session commits advance it forward. `.claude` main stays frozen. At close-out, fast-forward `.claude` main to it. Plays nicely with current push tooling. `.claude` history stays linear.
- **(b) Keep `.claude` on main** — push advances `.claude` main as normal, code side advances `init-clone-refactor`. Requires asymmetric-bookmark push, which doesn't exist yet. Probably out of scope for this fork.
- **(c) Use `--scope` to split** — code-only push for branch work, periodic bot-only push for session capture. Awkward; not what scope was designed for.

I'd go with **(a)**. It's mechanically the simplest, doesn't require touching push, and "fast-forward .claude main to init-clone-refactor at close-out" is just `jj bookmark set main -r init-clone-refactor` — no rewrite. The asymmetric ordering between code and bot history (rebased main commits will point at .claude commits that come *before* their init-clone-refactor pairs) is a cosmetic wrinkle, not a chid-correctness problem.

Sound right? If so I'll lay out the exact jj commands as the next message.

I don't like mirror, it implies symetry the code and bot will no longer be symetrical|mirrors of each other. We need another name; "partner" bookmarks, "disjoint", "asymetric" suggestions ..

content.text:
Naming suggestions, in order of preference:

- **partner** — bookmarks cooperate via `push` but each holds its own repo's state. Conveys "yoked together for a purpose, not duplicates of each other." Pairs naturally with a verb form ("partnered bookmarks") and reads well in code/docs.
- **tandem** — captures "advance together via the same mechanism, but each carries its own load." Like a tandem bicycle — two seats, one drivetrain. Slightly more evocative than partner, slightly less common in software vocabulary.
- **paired** — neutral and clear; weaker than partner because "pair" can imply matching halves.

I'd avoid:

- **mirror** — yes, exactly the problem you flagged.
- **disjoint** / **asymmetric** — these describe a property of the relationship, not the technique. Good for prose ("the bookmarks are disjoint here"), poor as a label.
- **yoked** — accurate metaphor but obscure; readers will stumble.

My pick is **partner**, with **tandem** as runner-up. Both leave room to talk about "partner-bookmark mode" or "tandem push" when documenting later.

Once you pick, I'll write the plan capture for `0.42.0-5`. The capture should land in `notes/chores-07.md` (it's about the 0.42.0 cycle's branching choice) and define the term for future reference. Want me to wait for your call on the name, then proceed?

Thoughts on changing descrption of 0.42.0-4 to 0.42.0-pm as it has nothing really to do 0.42.0 ladder except for adding "fmt::Display for Scope" we could revert that, change descrption and do the "dangerous" force push. That would be "cleaner" but dangerous, what if we just change the description is it still a force push I think so but not sure?

content.text:
## Force-push question

Yes, even a description-only change is a force push. `jj describe` rewrites the commit — same `change_id` (good, preserves ochid validity), new `commit_id`. The bookmark moves to the new `commit_id`. From git's view that's a non-fast-forward bookmark move, which is force-push territory. Same is true of `jj squash`, `jj rebase`, any rewrite. Only operations that don't change the commit_id are FF-safe (and there basically aren't any for an existing commit — the commit_id is a hash of its content).

Side note on `-pm`: per CLAUDE.md the suffix is numeric specifically for semver ordering (`0.42.0-1 < 0.42.0-2 < … < 0.42.0`). `-pm` is non-numeric, which semver compares lexically — `0.42.0-2 < 0.42.0-pm < 0.42.0`, sorts *after* every numeric ladder rung. Not broken, but it breaks the convention. If you want an "out of sequence" marker, a high numeric (like `0.42.0-99`) preserves the rule; or just keep `-4` in Cargo.toml and signal the out-of-bandness in the description.

## Should we do it?

I'd lean toward **leaving 55eadc8 alone**. Reasoning:

- The "uncleanliness" is temporary: at close-out we rebase main onto init-clone-refactor and drop 55eadc8 in the rebase (its content has been re-landed). So the cosmetic mess self-resolves.
- Force-pushing 55eadc8 *now* is the first time we'd be exercising chid-preservation-across-rewrite on this project for real. That's a thing we'd want to test deliberately, not as a side effect of cleanup.
- The `fmt::Display for Scope` addition that snuck in — if it's tiny and inert on main (no callers), leaving it costs nothing; if it'd conflict with the eventual 0.42.0 work, we can revert it as a tiny standalone commit later.
- Two operations (rewrite 55eadc8 + plan capture for 0.42.0-5) vs one (just the plan capture) — the simpler path puts us at the fork faster.

The case *for* the rewrite is real if `fmt::Display for Scope` is going to interfere with later 0.42.0 work, or if you want a clean main while init-clone-refactor is in flight (psychological tidiness has value too).

Your call. If you want the rewrite, I'd suggest `0.42.0-99` over `-pm` to keep the semver convention intact.

On the code and probably bot side another change I think will be necessary is multiple ochids on a commit so a "viewer" app showing code and bot conversations can keep them in sync. Thoughts!

content.text:
Big topic. A few framings I think actually hold up:

## Bot repo as a forest of linear chains

The "linear" property generalizes — each session is one append-only chain, and the bot repo is a forest of those chains, never merged. No bot-side merge commits at all. Code-side has a DAG (merges, branches); bot-side is a forest. The mapping is the multi-ochid trailer set on each code commit, fanning out to the contributing chain tips.

This is a cleaner model than "bot side mirrors code side topology" because:
- Each conversation is what it is — merging two conversations into one commit would be falsifying the historical record. A jsonl line is a wire-format record of "the user said X at time T," and merging that with another user's "I said Y at time T'" produces an event that never happened.
- "Never rewrite bot commits" becomes an enforceable rule rather than a guideline. ochid-into-bot is automatically stable.
- Storage and sync stay simple — appending only, no merge conflict resolution on bot side ever.

## Multi-line ochid trailer

Standard git trailer convention already allows multiple values for the same key. Just emit `ochid:` once per pointer:

```
ochid: /.claude/abc123
ochid: /.claude/def456
ochid: /.claude/ghi789
```

Parsers (`vc-x1 chid`, viewers) collect all instances. Single-pointer commits stay backward compatible — they're just the N=1 case. No version bump needed; existing parsers either already accept repeated trailers or are easy to extend.

## When multi-ochid actually shows up

- **Code-side merge with no extra bot work** — N ochids = the merge parents' contributing tip chids (one per parent). Possibly with de-duplication if parents share ancestry.
- **Code-side merge with conflict-resolution session** — N parents' ochids + the resolution session's tip ochid. So if user A and user B merge into M and user C resolves the conflict, M has 3 ochids: A's tip, B's tip, C's session tip.
- **Single-user single-session** — still 1 ochid. Same as today.
- **Code rebase** — ochids unchanged (chids are stable). Same as today. Multi-user rebases work the same as single-user — preserved chids preserved trailers.

## Per-user vs shared bot repo

Open question worth deciding before code lands:

- **Shared bot repo** (today's model extended): one `.claude/` repo holds all sessions across all users. Push from any user appends a chain. Pros: viewer sees everything in one place; cross-references resolve locally. Cons: every user pulls every other user's session data on sync — could be heavy. Permissions are coarse.
- **Per-user bot repos**: each user has their own `.claude-<user>/` repo, ochid trailers carry a user qualifier (`ochid: /.claude-alice/abc123`). Pros: lightweight sync, natural permissions. Cons: viewer needs to know all the bot-repo URLs to show full context; offline viewing breaks if you don't have the relevant user's repo.

I'd lean **shared** as the default with a per-project escape hatch — most teams want the cross-references to "just work." The qualifier in ochid (the path component after the leading `/`) can already encode either model: `/.claude/<chid>` for shared, `/.claude-alice/<chid>` for per-user. The trailer format itself is forward-compatible.

## Things I'd want to think about more before committing

- **Conflict resolution sessions: how do they get authored?** When user C resolves a merge conflict, does their bot session start a fresh chain rooted "from nowhere," or branch off one of the contributing chains? The chain-rooting question affects how the viewer renders it.
- **De-duplication on octopus merges.** If parents share ancestry, you'll get redundant ochids. Trim to unique tips? Keep all? Probably trim, but worth deciding.
- **Bot-side bookmark scheme.** Per-session bookmark? Per-user-per-feature? Naming convention matters because bookmarks are how `jj git push` decides what to send.
- **Viewer concerns.** "Show me the sessions that contributed to this commit" is straightforward (read the trailers, walk back in each chain to the relevant range). "Show me the code commits this session contributed to" is the inverse and needs an index, since chids don't carry forward references. Probably build the index from a one-time scan of code-side trailers.
- **Migration story for existing repos** with single-ochid trailers — they're already valid in the multi-ochid scheme (N=1 case), so probably no migration needed. Worth confirming on the parser side.

Big picture: I think the multi-ochid + linear-forest bot repo + immutable bot commits model is coherent and scales the existing scheme cleanly. The hard parts are mostly UX (viewer, bot bookmark scheme) rather than data-model issues. Worth capturing as a chores section sometime — but probably not in the 0.42.0-5 plan capture, since that's about the immediate fork, not the multi-user roadmap.

1	# vc-x1 init validation notes
2	
3	Empirical observations from testing `vc-x1 init --scope` at
4	0.42.0-3, focused on anomalies that need follow-up. Structural
5	correctness confirmed for each tested case unless noted.
6	
7	## Test setup
8	
9	- Binary: `vc-x1 0.42.0-3`
10	- Test root: `../tmp-vc-x1/` (sibling of project dir)
11	- Session: 2026-04-27
12	
13	## Test 1: `--scope=code`
14	
15	`vc-x1 init repoA --repo-local=../tmp-vc-x1 --scope=code`
16	
17	Single-repo workspace produced correctly: no `.claude/`,
18	config has `path = "/"` only, no `other-repo`.
19	
20	Anomalies:
21	
22	- `bm-track exit` line prints `app(main)=tracked,
23	  .claude(main)=tracked` even though no `.claude/` exists in
24	  single-repo mode. The bot thinks the bm-track wrapper format
25	  is hard-coded for the dual-repo case.
26	- Step 4 has no console announcement (output jumps 3 → 5).
27	  Cosmetic only.
28	- "Done!" footer labels the bare remote as `Code repo:`. The
29	  shown path is `remote.git` (bare), not the working repo at
30	  `repoA`. Misleading label — should distinguish working repo
31	  vs bare remote.
32	
33	## Test 2: `--scope=code,bot`
34	
35	`vc-x1 init repoA --repo-local=../tmp-vc-x1 --scope=code,bot`
36	
37	Dual-repo workspace produced correctly: both `.vc-config.toml`
38	files have expected shape (`path` + `other-repo`), both initial
39	commits carry cross-referencing `ochid:` trailers, symlink at
40	`~/.claude/projects/...repoA` resolves to a real directory.
41	
42	Anomalies:
43	
44	- Same labeling issue as Test 1: "Done!" footer labels both
45	  bares as `Code repo:` / `Session repo:`. Paths shown are
46	  `remote-code.git` / `remote-claude.git` (bares), not the
47	  working repos.
48	- Symlink target embeds the literal `..` from the
49	  `--repo-local` invocation:
50	  `/home/wink/data/prgs/rust/vc-x1/../tmp-vc-x1/repoA/.claude`.
51	  Functional (filesystem resolves `..`) but not canonicalized.
52	  The bot thinks `init` passes `--repo-local` verbatim into
53	  the symlink target rather than calling `canonicalize` first.
54	
55	## Test 3: `--scope=bot,code`
56	
57	`vc-x1 init repoA --repo-local=../tmp-vc-x1 --scope=bot,code`
58	
59	Verified structurally identical to Test 2: same `.vc-config.toml`
60	contents in both repos, same ochid cross-reference pattern (each
61	initial commit points at the other's chid), same symlink shape.
62	Order-of-sides in the keyword form is commutative for init's
63	purposes.
64	
65	No new anomalies.
66	
67	## Test 4: `--scope=bot` from a clean dir
68	
69	`vc-x1 init repoA --repo-local=../tmp-vc-x1 --scope=bot`
70	
71	Expected fatal — confirmed:
72	
73	- Exit 1.
74	- Error: `--scope=bot is meaningless at init time — use
75	  --scope=code for single-repo or --scope=code,bot (default)
76	  for dual-repo`.
77	- Filesystem unchanged (no `repoA/` created).
78	- `bm-track exit` line still prints
79	  `app(main)=tracked, .claude(main)=tracked` even though the
80	  init aborted before touching any repo. Same cosmetic anomaly
81	  as Test 1 (the bm-track wrapper format appears scope-blind).
82	
83	## Test 5: `--scope=code` after a failed bot attempt
84	
85	`vc-x1 init repoA --repo-local=../tmp-vc-x1 --scope=code`
86	
87	Same outcome as Test 1 (single-repo workspace produced
88	correctly). Confirms the failed Test 4 attempt left no
89	residual state that interferes with the subsequent
90	single-repo init.
91	
92	## Test 6: `--scope=bot` on top of existing code-only workspace
93	
94	`vc-x1 init repoA --repo-local=../tmp-vc-x1 --scope=bot`
95	(after Test 5 left a code-only workspace in place)
96	
97	Expected: ideally an incremental upgrade — add `.claude/`,
98	rewrite parent's `.vc-config.toml` to include `other-repo`,
99	ochid-link the existing initial commit to a new bot-side
100	initial commit, create the symlink. Actual:
101	
102	- Same plan-time error as Test 4 — the
103	  `--scope=bot is meaningless` check fires before any
104	  inspection of the existing workspace.
105	- Filesystem unchanged.
106	- The error message suggests `--scope=code,bot` as the
107	  alternative, but that path is also blocked (see Test 7).
108	
109	## Test 7: `--scope=code,bot` on top of existing code-only workspace
110	
111	`vc-x1 init repoA --repo-local=../tmp-vc-x1 --scope=code,bot`
112	(after Test 5 left a code-only workspace in place)
113	
114	Expected fatal — confirmed, but at a different check than
115	Test 6:
116	
117	- Exit 1.
118	- Error fires at preflight (after `Preflight checks...`):
119	  `'/home/wink/data/prgs/rust/vc-x1/../tmp-vc-x1/repoA'
120	  already exists`.
121	- Filesystem unchanged.
122	
123	## Findings: missing incremental-upgrade path
124	
125	`init` today supports two and only two terminal modes from a
126	clean dir:
127	
128	| Mode | Invocation | Outcome |
129	|---|---|---|
130	| Single-repo | `--scope=code` | code repo + `.vc-config.toml` (no `other-repo`) |
131	| Dual-repo | `--scope=code,bot` (or default) | both repos + cross-ref configs + ochid trailers + symlink |
132	
133	There is **no path** to grow a single-repo workspace into a
134	dual-repo workspace via `init`. Both candidate invocations
135	fail:
136	
137	- `init --scope=bot` on existing code-only → fatal at
138	  plan-time (`--scope=bot is meaningless at init time`),
139	  before any inspection of the existing workspace.
140	- `init --scope=code,bot` on existing code-only → fatal at
141	  preflight (`'<path>/repoA' already exists`).
142	
143	The bot's design sketch for the upgrade path:
144	
145	- Detect the upgrade case in plan-time: `--scope=bot` AND the
146	  target dir contains a `.vc-config.toml` with `path = "/"`
147	  AND no `other-repo`.
148	- Switch to upgrade mode: skip code-side init steps; create
149	  `.claude/`, write `.claude/.vc-config.toml`, rewrite
150	  parent's `.vc-config.toml` to add `other-repo = ".claude"`,
151	  bare-init the bot remote, write a bot-side initial commit
152	  with an `ochid:` trailer pointing at the existing code
153	  initial commit, optionally rewrite the existing code commit
154	  to add the reverse `ochid:` trailer (or accept asymmetry
155	  for the upgrade case), create the symlink.
156	- `init --scope=code,bot` on existing code-only could either
157	  also enter upgrade mode (reusing the existing code repo) or
158	  stay fatal — the user-facing semantics matter (does
159	  `code,bot` mean "ensure both" or "create both fresh"?). The
160	  bot's lean: keep `code,bot` strict-create (today's
161	  semantics) and route the upgrade through the explicit
162	  `--scope=bot`-on-existing form.
163	
164	This is a feature gap, not a bug — to be filed as a todo
165	candidate after the 0.41.1 sync work is settled.
166	
167	## Test 8: `--scope=<path>` (path-form)
168	
169	`vc-x1 init repoA --scope=../tmp-vc-x1-path`
170	(no `--repo-local`, no `--repo-remote`)
171	
172	Expected fatal — confirmed:
173	
174	- Exit 1.
175	- Error: `--scope=../tmp-vc-x1-path: path-form scope is
176	  meaningless at init time — use --repo-local <PATH> for a
177	  local fixture or --repo-remote <URL> for an existing
178	  remote`.
179	- Filesystem unchanged (no `repoA/` or `tmp-vc-x1-path/`
180	  created).
181	- Same `bm-track` cosmetic anomaly fires.
182	
183	Status: per the chores-07 design (`### --scope enum refactor
184	(0.42.0)`'s applicability matrix), init was deliberately
185	specified as `Single(_)` accepted = no, on the rationale
186	"init creates workspaces, not POR repos." Re-opened
187	2026-04-27 — see "Findings: path-form support" below.
188	
189	## Findings: path-form support is wanted
190	
191	The user wants `init --scope=<path>` to work. The current
192	flag set splits two concerns that the path form could fold
193	together:
194	
195	| Today | Path-form shorthand |
196	|---|---|
197	| `--scope=code` (mode) + `--repo-local=<dir>` (location) | `--scope=<dir>` |
198	
199	Two reasonable semantics for `init NAME --scope=<path>`:
200	
201	- **(a) Local-fixture single-repo shorthand.** Equivalent
202	  to `init NAME --repo-local=<path> --scope=code`. Creates
203	  a single-repo workspace at `<path>/NAME` with a local bare
204	  remote, no GitHub. Mirrors the `Single(_)` semantic from
205	  sync — "ignore the dual-repo defaults, operate on this one
206	  spot." This is the bot's recommended reading.
207	- **(b) Bare-init at exactly `<path>`.** The path IS the
208	  workspace location, no `NAME` segment appended. Conflicts
209	  with the existing `NAME` positional arg (would have to
210	  become optional / mutually exclusive). More invasive.
211	
212	Reading (a) is the cleanest: it preserves the existing
213	`NAME` positional, drops to single-repo by default
214	(consistent with `Single(_)` everywhere else), and the
215	generated workspace looks identical to what
216	`--scope=code --repo-local=<path>` produces today.
217	
218	The user's stated priority (2026-04-27): implement
219	path-form support **before** the 0.41.1 sync fix, since the
220	sync fix's validation harness benefits from the shorthand.
221
