content.content:
32	### Command structure
33	
34	```
35	vc-x1 init  <TARGET> [NAME] [--scope code,bot|por] [--private] [--dry-run]
36	vc-x1 clone <TARGET> [NAME] [--scope code,bot|por]             [--dry-run]
37	```
38	
39	Identical surfaces modulo `--private` (init only).
40	
41	`<TARGET>` accepts:
42	
43	- **URL** — `git@host:owner/name(.git)?`,
44	  `https://...(.git)?` (detected by `://` or SSH
45	  `<host>:<path>` shape with `@`).
46	- **`owner/name` shorthand** — single `/`, no path prefix;
47	  resolved to `git@github.com:owner/name.git`.
48	- **Path-prefixed** — `./X`, `../X`, `/X`, `~/X`, `~`,
49	  bare `.`, or bare `..`. Path IS the target; last component
50	  is the workspace name (consumer canonicalizes `.` / `..`
51	  to a real basename via `canonicalize` + `file_name`).
52	- **Bare alphanumeric NAME** — for **init** only, expands to
53	  `<owner>/NAME` via the user config
54	  ([`User config`](#user-config-0411-3)). Missing config →
55	  error with hint to use `owner/name` shorthand or set
56	  `[default].remote-provider` + `[github].owner`. For
57	  **clone**, bare NAME is an error (genuinely ambiguous —
58	  "missing `./`?" / "missing `/name` suffix?"); clone has
59	  no config-driven defaults.
60	
61	`[NAME]`:
62	
63	- **init**: URL / `owner/name` forms → overrides the derived
64	  workspace name (workspace at `cwd/<NAME>`). Path forms →
65	  error if combined (path already specifies the destination).
66	- **clone**: all forms (URL, `owner/name`, Path-as-source) →
67	  destination dir name in cwd. Without `[NAME]`, derived
68	  from the source via `derive_name`. For clone, TARGET is
69	  the *source* (URL or local repo path) and `[NAME]` is
70	  the *destination* — symmetric with `git clone <src>
71	  [<dest>]`.
72	
73	`--scope`:
74	
75	- `code,bot` (default): both repos, dual-repo layout.
76	- `por`: single repo, no `.vc-config.toml` written.
77	- `code` / `bot` standalone: error. Reason — these are
78	  config-lookup keywords; init has no config to look up
79	  against, and clone's simplification omits them too. The
80	  manual decomposition (clone code as POR, clone bot as
81	  POR, place at `.claude/`, run `vc-x1 symlink`) covers the
82	  rare cases where a user wants the dual layout composed
83	  by hand.
84	
85	`--private`: only meaningful for init when creating a new
86	GitHub repo. Sets visibility at `gh repo create`. Silently
87	warn-and-ignore if the remote already exists. Errors on

file.content:
### Command structure

```
vc-x1 init  <TARGET> [NAME] [--scope code,bot|por] [--private] [--dry-run]
vc-x1 clone <TARGET> [NAME] [--scope code,bot|por]             [--dry-run]
```

Identical surfaces modulo `--private` (init only).

`<TARGET>` accepts:

- **URL** — `git@host:owner/name(.git)?`,
  `https://...(.git)?` (detected by `://` or SSH
  `<host>:<path>` shape with `@`).
- **`owner/name` shorthand** — single `/`, no path prefix;
  resolved to `git@github.com:owner/name.git`.
- **Path-prefixed** — `./X`, `../X`, `/X`, `~/X`, `~`,
  bare `.`, or bare `..`. Path IS the target; last component
  is the workspace name (consumer canonicalizes `.` / `..`
  to a real basename via `canonicalize` + `file_name`).
- **Bare alphanumeric NAME** — for **init** only, expands to
  `<owner>/NAME` via the user config
  ([`User config`](#user-config-0411-3)). Missing config →
  error with hint to use `owner/name` shorthand or set
  `[default].remote-provider` + `[github].owner`. For
  **clone**, bare NAME is an error (genuinely ambiguous —
  "missing `./`?" / "missing `/name` suffix?"); clone has
  no config-driven defaults.

`[NAME]`:

- **init**: URL / `owner/name` forms → overrides the derived
  workspace name (workspace at `cwd/<NAME>`). Path forms →
  error if combined (path already specifies the destination).
- **clone**: all forms (URL, `owner/name`, Path-as-source) →
  destination dir name in cwd. Without `[NAME]`, derived
  from the source via `derive_name`. For clone, TARGET is
  the *source* (URL or local repo path) and `[NAME]` is
  the *destination* — symmetric with `git clone <src>
  [<dest>]`.

`--scope`:

- `code,bot` (default): both repos, dual-repo layout.
- `por`: single repo, no `.vc-config.toml` written.
- `code` / `bot` standalone: error. Reason — these are
  config-lookup keywords; init has no config to look up
  against, and clone's simplification omits them too. The
  manual decomposition (clone code as POR, clone bot as
  POR, place at `.claude/`, run `vc-x1 symlink`) covers the
  rare cases where a user wants the dual layout composed
  by hand.

`--private`: only meaningful for init when creating a new
GitHub repo. Sets visibility at `gh repo create`. Silently
warn-and-ignore if the remote already exists. Errors on
clone (clone never creates a remote).

`--dry-run`: validate the operation as far as possible
without side effects. Print what would be done.

message.content: Another alternative define "--repo-{remote|local} the-remote" parameter for all subcommands that interact with repos. The default could be in .vc-config.toml or `~/.config/vc-x1/config.toml and "the-remote" would be taken from the config file depending on which you wanted. Thoughts?

content.text:
Want to make sure I understand before designing — a few interpretations of your proposal:

**Reading 1 (symmetric flags, named configs):**
```toml
[remote.github-personal]
provider = "github"
owner = "winksaville"
[remote.github-work]
provider = "github"
owner = "anthropic"

[local.scratch]
parent = "/tmp/scratch"
[local.test-fixtures]
parent = "~/test-fixtures"

[default]
repo-remote = "github-personal"
repo-local = "test-fixtures"
```

CLI:
- `vc-x1 init tf1` → uses `[default].repo-remote` → `[remote.github-personal]`
- `vc-x1 init tf1 --repo-remote github-work` → uses `[remote.github-work]`
- `vc-x1 init tf1 --repo-local scratch` → uses `[local.scratch]` (fixture mode)
- `vc-x1 init tf1 --repo-local` (no value) → uses default `[local.test-fixtures]`

The "the-remote" arg is a **name** that keys into the config. Adds named-config capability (multi-account, multi-fixture-parent).

**Reading 2 (symmetric flags, value is path-or-URL directly):**
```
vc-x1 init tf1 --repo-remote git@github.com:wink/tf1.git
vc-x1 init tf1 --repo-local /tmp/scratch
```
Value is the literal target, not a config name. Config holds defaults only.

Reading 1 is more capable (named configs), Reading 2 is closer to my current `--fixture [PATH]` just renamed. Which did you have in mind?

**Three design questions either way:**

1. **One-off paths.** Under Reading 1, how do I pass a path that isn't in config? Heuristic ("looks like a path" vs alphanumeric name)? Separate flag (`--repo-local-path`)? Or only support what's in config?

2. **Cross-subcommand semantics.** You said "all subcommands that interact with repos." For `vc-x1 sync` / `push` (which operate on already-configured remotes via `.git/config`), what does `--repo-remote foo` *mean*? Override the push target? Pick among multiple remotes if a workspace has more than one? That's a meaningful design question — these flags currently mean "which side(s)" (`code`/`bot`/`code,bot`/`<path>`), not "which named provider."

3. **Scope for 0.41.1.** Named configs add real complexity (config-section-per-name, lookup, validation). I could ship the simpler symmetric flags now and add named-config lookup in 0.41.2 — or commit to the bigger design upfront. Which fits your timing?

message.content: I was thinking #1 which you've generalized a little more which is quite nice capability. The thing we need at a minimum we use two different repo locations, remote or local. But in actual fact there could be N different repo locations and you it'd be nice to specify them with abrevations and also have a default, which for me would be `github.com` and if I'm at work there could be project specifc repos as well as my personel one, so supporting N is the best approach. We should allow one additional generalization --repo-<key> assumed keys are local and repo but user can add anything else!

content.text:
Got it — N named configs, partitioned by category, with `local` and `remote` as starter categories but user can add their own.

**Proposed schema:**
```toml
# Each [repo.<category>.<name>] is a named config block
[repo.remote.github-personal]
provider = "github"
owner = "winksaville"

[repo.remote.github-work]
provider = "github"
owner = "anthropic"

[repo.local.scratch]
parent = "/tmp/scratch"

[repo.local.test-fixtures]
parent = "~/test-fixtures"

# user-defined category (e.g. staging)
[repo.staging.preview]
provider = "github"
owner = "wink-preview"

# defaults per category
[default]
repo-remote = "github-personal"
repo-local = "test-fixtures"
```

`[repo.<category>.<name>]` is the data; `[default].repo-<category>` picks the default within a category. Categories are arbitrary strings — `local` and `remote` are blessed but anything works.

**CLI design — pick one:**

**Option D1 — dynamic `--repo-<category> [name]` flag:**
```
vc-x1 init tf1                                    # uses [default].repo-remote (fallback to "remote" category)
vc-x1 init tf1 --repo-remote github-work          # uses [repo.remote.github-work]
vc-x1 init tf1 --repo-local scratch               # uses [repo.local.scratch]
vc-x1 init tf1 --repo-local                       # uses [default].repo-local
vc-x1 init tf1 --repo-staging preview             # user-defined category
```
- Reads naturally; matches your sketch exactly.
- Implementation: pre-parse argv, pluck `--repo-*` flags into a map, pass remainder to clap.
- Cost: ~50 LOC of pre-parsing + lose clap's built-in help/completion for these flags (could compensate by listing known categories in `--help`).

**Option D2 — single `--repo <cat>=<name>` flag (clap-native):**
```
vc-x1 init tf1 --repo remote=github-work
vc-x1 init tf1 --repo local=scratch
vc-x1 init tf1 --repo local                       # uses default for "local"
vc-x1 init tf1 --repo staging=preview
```
- Clap-native, no pre-parse.
- Slightly less ergonomic; wordier syntax.
- Cost: ~20 LOC, repeatable flag with custom value parser.

**Two scope questions:**

1. **For -4, init only or all repo-touching subcommands?** I'd lean init-only for -4; add to sync/push/etc. in a follow-on cycle once we see the pattern shake out. Per-subcommand wiring is small but spreads the diff wide.

2. **0.41.1-3 already shipped a config schema; do we evolve it now (rename `[default].repo-remote-provider` → `[default].repo-remote`, restructure into `[repo.<cat>.<name>]`) or keep old schema as a transitional step?** Since -3's module has no consumers yet, I'd just rewrite the schema cleanly — no migration baggage.

Which CLI option (D1 or D2)? And confirm scope (init-only for now)?

message.content: D2 seems more "obvious" if already supported, but we need to specify what `--repo` the default if there is no argument to repo. And also what the default is when there is `--repo` parameter.
D2 seems more "obvious" if already supported, but we need to specify what the default is when using `--repo` with no parameter and if also when it's missing but required.

content.text:
Resolution rules with explicit defaults / error messages:

**1. `--repo` absent entirely:**
- Look up `[default].repo-remote` → `<name>`.
- Then resolve `[repo.remote.<name>]`.
- `[default].repo-remote` unset → error: `no repo specified; pass --repo <cat>=<name> or set [default].repo-remote in ~/.config/vc-x1/config.toml`.
- `[repo.remote.<name>]` missing → error: `[default].repo-remote = "<name>" but [repo.remote.<name>] section not found`.

(Why `repo-remote` as the global default? Init's dominant case is "create a github remote." If you want fixtures-by-default, set `[default].repo-remote = "<some-local-name>"` — note the `repo-remote` key still gates the absent-flag path; the *category* is encoded via the resolved section, not the default key. Or we add a separate `[default].repo` key for "the absolute default category"; lmk preference.)

**2. `--repo <cat>` (no `=name`):**
- Look up `[default].repo-<cat>` → `<name>`.
- Then resolve `[repo.<cat>.<name>]`.
- `[default].repo-<cat>` unset → error: `no default for category '<cat>'; set [default].repo-<cat> or use --repo <cat>=<name>`.
- `[repo.<cat>.<name>]` missing → same "section not found" error as above.

**3. `--repo <cat>=<name>` (explicit):**
- Resolve `[repo.<cat>.<name>]` directly.
- Section missing → error: `config section [repo.<cat>.<name>] not found`.

**4. Multiple `--repo` flags:**
- Error: `multiple --repo specified; init creates one repo at a time`. (Init's dispatch is one-shot — you're either making a github remote OR local bares, not both.)

**5. Config file absent entirely + `--repo` absent:**
- Same as (1)'s "[default].repo-remote unset" branch — error points at the config file.

**6. Category dispatch (which fields to expect in section):**
- `remote` category → reads `provider` + `owner` (+ optional `service`).
- `local` category → reads `parent`.
- Any other category in -4 → error `unknown category '<cat>' (built-ins: remote, local)`.
  Future cycle adds a `kind = "remote" | "local"` field for user-defined categories. Schema-forward-compatible since unknown categories already error in -4.

**Open question — global default key:**

Two readings of "the default when --repo is absent":

- **(a)** Use `[default].repo-remote` (the remote-category default). What I described above. Predictable but assumes "remote" is the meta-default.
- **(b)** Add an explicit `[default].repo = "<cat>=<name>"` key for the absolute default. More explicit but adds another knob.

I lean (a) — fewer config keys, matches the "init creates a github remote" common case. (b) is more pedantic. Your call.

Want me to start the implementation with these rules?

message.content: (a) is good, but I think the user should be able to choose the default <cat> key, "local" or "remote" or their own "cat". maybe that's in (2.) above?

content.text:
Yes, exactly — three-level config so the user controls the fallback category. Here's the cleaned-up resolution:

**Schema (adds one key):**
```toml
[default]
repo = "remote"                  # category to use when --repo is absent (NEW)
repo-remote = "github-personal"  # default name within "remote"
repo-local = "scratch"           # default name within "local"

[repo.remote.github-personal]
provider = "github"
owner = "winksaville"

[repo.local.scratch]
parent = "/tmp/scratch"
```

**Resolution chain — same shape at all three levels:**

| Invocation | Step 1 (find category) | Step 2 (find name) | Step 3 (resolve section) |
|---|---|---|---|
| (no `--repo`) | `[default].repo` → `<cat>` | `[default].repo-<cat>` → `<name>` | `[repo.<cat>.<name>]` |
| `--repo <cat>` | `<cat>` (from CLI) | `[default].repo-<cat>` → `<name>` | `[repo.<cat>.<name>]` |
| `--repo <cat>=<name>` | `<cat>` (from CLI) | `<name>` (from CLI) | `[repo.<cat>.<name>]` |

Each step that consults config has its own missing-key error pointing at exactly which key needs setting:
- Step 1 missing → `no repo specified; set [default].repo or use --repo <cat>=<name>`.
- Step 2 missing → `no default for category '<cat>'; set [default].repo-<cat> or use --repo <cat>=<name>`.
- Step 3 missing → `config section [repo.<cat>.<name>] not found`.

So a fixture-default user just sets:
```toml
[default]
repo = "local"
repo-local = "test-fixtures"

[repo.local.test-fixtures]
parent = "~/test-fixtures"
```

Then `vc-x1 init tf1` defaults to fixture mode without further flags. Symmetric with `repo = "remote"` for the GitHub-default user.

OK to lock this in and start building?

lastPrompt: (a) is good, but I think the user should be able to choose the default <cat> key, "local" or "remote" or their own "cat". maybe that's in (2.) above?

message.content: I was leaning toward `--remote <cat>=<name>` supported by clap natively as I thought that was more well known, but you seem to indicate I favored the "-" style.

content.text:
Sorry, no — I've been using D2 syntax throughout. The table in my last message uses `--repo <cat>` and `--repo <cat>=<name>`, the clap-native single-flag form. We're on the same page.

One small thing — you wrote `--remote <cat>=<name>` (singular `--remote`). Was that a typo for `--repo`, or are you suggesting the flag name itself should be `--remote`?

I'd lean `--repo`:
- The flag references any category (`remote`, `local`, `staging`, ...), so naming it after one specific category (`--remote`) reads oddly: `--remote local=scratch` is confusing.
- `--repo remote=github-work` keeps the flag generic; the category lives in the value where it belongs.

If you meant `--repo`, we're locked in. Confirm and I'll start building.

message.content:
Above I see:
[default].repo-<cat> → <name>

And
[default]
repo = "local"
repo-local = "~/test-fixtures"

Hence it seems you suggesting `--repo-local` as the way to define the <cat>

content.text:
Good catch — you're right that it's inconsistent. The config key `[default].repo-<cat>` naturally reads as "default for `--repo-<cat>`", which implies D1 (dynamic `--repo-local`/`--repo-remote` flags). With D2 (`--repo cat=name`) those config keys don't match a flag.

Two ways to fix:

**Fix A — switch to D1 (dynamic flags), keep schema as drafted:**
```toml
[default]
repo = "local"                     # absolute default (no flag at all)
repo-local = "test-fixtures"       # default for --repo-local
repo-remote = "github-personal"    # default for --repo-remote
```
- Config-key-name == flag-name, zero translation.
- CLI: `--repo-local`, `--repo-remote`, `--repo-staging`, … any category.
- Cost: ~50 LOC of argv pre-parsing; lose clap's auto-completion for `--repo-*`.

**Fix B — keep D2, rename config keys so they don't look like flags:**
```toml
[default]
repo-cat = "local"                 # default category
repo-name-local = "test-fixtures"  # default name within "local"
repo-name-remote = "github-personal"
```
- CLI stays clap-native: `--repo cat=name`, `--repo cat`, `--repo` absent.
- Config keys (`repo-cat`, `repo-name-<cat>`) read as distinctly-config-side, not as flags.

I think your instinct was actually right — Fix A (D1) is more ergonomic because the config keys and CLI flags speak the same language. The implementation cost is real but bounded. D2's clap-native simplicity is offset by needing the `repo-name-<cat>` translation that nobody types.

My lean: **Fix A (D1)**. Want to commit?

content.text:
Yes, makes sense. Locking in:

**CLI:** D2 native — `--repo <cat>=<name>` (clap-parsed).

**Config (going natural since you gave me free rein):**
```toml
[default]
category = "remote"          # used when --repo is absent

[default.name]
remote = "github-personal"   # used when --repo remote (no name)
local = "test-fixtures"      # used when --repo local (no name)

[repo.remote.github-personal]
provider = "github"
owner = "winksaville"

[repo.local.test-fixtures]
parent = "~/test-fixtures"
```

Config-side names (`[default].category`, `[default.name].<cat>`) don't look like CLI flags, so no confusion. Three layers, each with its own default-finding key.

**Resolution:**

| CLI | Step 1: category | Step 2: name | Step 3: section |
|---|---|---|---|
| (absent) | `[default].category` | `[default.name].<cat>` | `[repo.<cat>.<name>]` |
| `--repo <cat>` | `<cat>` (CLI) | `[default.name].<cat>` | `[repo.<cat>.<name>]` |
| `--repo <cat>=<name>` | `<cat>` (CLI) | `<name>` (CLI) | `[repo.<cat>.<name>]` |

Each missing-config error points at the exact key to set.

Starting implementation.

content.text:
Good catch — `category` is too generic. Scoping under `[default.repo]` keeps it clear and leaves `[default.<other-thing>]` open for future defaults:

```toml
[default.repo]
category = "remote"          # default category for --repo

[default.repo.name]
remote = "github-personal"   # default name when --repo remote (no name)
local = "test-fixtures"      # default name when --repo local (no name)

[repo.remote.github-personal]
provider = "github"
owner = "winksaville"

[repo.local.test-fixtures]
parent = "~/test-fixtures"
```

Now `category` reads as "the default category *for repos*", unambiguously. Future defaults like `[default.editor]`, `[default.log-level]`, etc. all sit under `[default.<thing>]` with their own keys — symmetric.

Lookup keys at each step:
- Step 1 (no `--repo`): `default.repo.category`
- Step 2 (no name): `default.repo.name.<cat>`
- Step 3: `repo.<cat>.<name>`

content.text:
Got it — pull `category` out into its own section. If I read your sketch correctly:

```toml
[default.repo]
category = "remote"          # absolute default category

[category.repo.name]
remote = "github-personal"   # default name when --repo remote (no name)
local = "test-fixtures"
work = "github-work"

[repo.remote.github-personal]
provider = "github"
owner = "winksaville"

[repo.local.test-fixtures]
parent = "~/test-fixtures"

[repo.work.github-work]
provider = "github"
owner = "anthropic"
```

Three distinct namespaces, each with one job:
- `[default.*]` — absolute defaults (when no flag at all).
- `[category.*]` — per-category defaults (used by `--repo <cat>` no-name form).
- `[repo.<cat>.<name>]` — the actual config blocks.

`category` is now a real section heading, not a key wedged under `[default.repo]`.

**Lookup keys at each step:**
- Step 1: `default.repo.category`
- Step 2: `category.repo.name.<cat>`
- Step 3: `repo.<cat>.<name>.*`

A nit: `[category.repo.name]` — is the `.name` part intentional (in case other category-related defaults appear later), or did you just intend `[category.repo]`?
- `[category.repo].remote = "github-personal"` (flatter)
- `[category.repo.name].remote = "github-personal"` (room for `.kind`, `.alias`, etc. someday)

Either reads fine. Mild lean toward `[category.repo]` (flatter, simpler) unless you want headroom for more category attributes.

Want flat or with `.name`?

content.text:
Got `[category.repo]` flat. Quick check on what the values mean — you wrote:
```toml
[category.repo]
remote = "abc"
local = "~/test-fixtures"
```

The values look mixed-shape: `"abc"` reads as a config-name (pointing at `[repo.remote.abc]`); `"~/test-fixtures"` reads as a literal path. Three readings:

**(i) Always names** (what I assumed):
```toml
[category.repo]
remote = "abc"
local = "test-fixtures"            # name, not path

[repo.remote.abc]
provider = "github"
owner = "abc"

[repo.local.test-fixtures]
parent = "~/test-fixtures"
```
Symmetric. Defaults always indirect through the `[repo.<cat>.<name>]` sections. Even fixtures need a named entry.

**(ii) Always literal values** (no named-config layer):
```toml
[category.repo]
remote = "git@github.com:abc/PROJECT.git"   # literal URL or template
local = "~/test-fixtures"                    # literal path
```
Simpler — collapses the named-config layer entirely. Loses the multi-account capability.

**(iii) Asymmetric — names for `remote`, literal paths for `local`**:
- Justifiable because fixture mode rarely benefits from named configs (you just want a parent dir).
- But asymmetric, surprises users when adding a new category.

I'd guess you meant **(i)** and the `~/test-fixtures` was just a quick example (meant to be a name like `"test-fixtures"`). Confirm?

If you actually meant **(ii)**, that's a bigger simplification — the whole `[repo.<cat>.<name>]` subsystem goes away and we're back to `--repo cat=value` where `value` is the literal target. Cleaner but loses N-named-configs.

Which interpretation?

message.content: The thing that's missing and maybe why we're talking a little past each other is we need to add comments in the .toml info to relate directly to the option. So let's be concrete; define the toml file that defines `--repo remote=github.com` as the default if absent and `--repo local=~/test-fixtures` and both can be shortened to `--repo remote` and `--repo local`. And also let's define an option `--debug trace` or `--debug debug` with `--debug trace` as the default if `--debug` is used. Comments in the toml to solidify how each is derived from the information in the toml.

The thing that's missing and maybe why we're talking a little past each other and extending as we go. Please provide an example that supports multiple "accounts".  So let's be concrete; define the toml file that defines the following "home" and another set for "work": `--repo remote=github.com` as the default if absent and `--repo local=~/test-fixtures` and both can be shortened to `--repo remote` and `--repo local`. And also let's define an option `--debug trace` or `--debug debug` with `--debug trace` as the default if `--debug` is used. Comments in the toml to solidify how each is derived from the information in the toml. And what mechanism would we use to select the default account and how would we supply it on the command line.

content.text:
Concrete schema for home/work + `--debug`, with derivation comments:

```toml
# ~/.config/vc-x1/config.toml
#
# Three layers:
#   - [default.*]              absolute defaults (no flag at all)
#   - [account.<acct>.*]       per-account defaults
#   - [repo.<cat>.<name>]      actual config blocks (account-shared)

# ====================================================================
# Top-level defaults — used when the corresponding flag is absent
# ====================================================================

[default]
account = "home"   # --account absent → "home"
debug = "trace"    # --debug used without value → "trace"

# ====================================================================
# Account: home
# ====================================================================

# --repo absent entirely:
#   [account.home.default.repo].category = "remote"
#   → [account.home.category.repo].remote = "github-personal"
#   → [repo.remote.github-personal]
[account.home.default.repo]
category = "remote"

# --repo <cat> (no =name):
#   [account.home.category.repo].<cat> → name → [repo.<cat>.<name>]
[account.home.category.repo]
remote = "github-personal"   # --repo remote → [repo.remote.github-personal]
local  = "test-fixtures"     # --repo local  → [repo.local.test-fixtures]

# ====================================================================
# Account: work
# ====================================================================

[account.work.default.repo]
category = "remote"

[account.work.category.repo]
remote = "github-work"       # --repo remote (under --account work)
local  = "work-fixtures"     # --repo local  (under --account work)

# ====================================================================
# Named repo configs — account-shared, referenced by name from accounts
# ====================================================================

[repo.remote.github-personal]
provider = "github"
owner    = "winksaville"
service  = "github.com"      # optional, defaults to "github.com"

[repo.remote.github-work]
provider = "github"
owner    = "anthropic"
service  = "github.com"

[repo.local.test-fixtures]
parent = "~/test-fixtures"

[repo.local.work-fixtures]
parent = "/work/test-fixtures"
```

**CLI invocations and what they resolve to:**

| Command | Account | Cat | Name | Resolves to |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `vc-x1 init tf1` | home | remote | github-personal | `[repo.remote.github-personal]` |
| `vc-x1 init tf1 --account work` | work | remote | github-work | `[repo.remote.github-work]` |
| `vc-x1 init tf1 --repo remote` | home | remote | github-personal | same as no-flag (home default) |
| `vc-x1 init tf1 --account work --repo local` | work | local | work-fixtures | `[repo.local.work-fixtures]` |
| `vc-x1 init tf1 --repo remote=github-work` | home | remote | github-work | `[repo.remote.github-work]` (explicit name override) |
| `vc-x1 init tf1 --debug` | — | — | — | debug level = "trace" |
| `vc-x1 init tf1 --debug debug` | — | — | — | debug level = "debug" |

**Account selection mechanism:**
- `--account <name>` CLI flag — per-invocation override (highest priority).
- `[default].account` config key — system default.
- (Future option: `VC_X1_ACCOUNT` env var if you want a third precedence layer; deferred until needed.)

**One semantic to confirm:** the values at `[account.<acct>.category.repo]` are **names** that point to `[repo.<cat>.<name>]` sections — symmetric across categories, supports the multi-account named-config story.

If you'd rather have the values be the literal targets (Reading ii — `remote = "git@github.com:winksaville"`, `local = "~/test-fixtures"`), the schema collapses by half (no `[repo.<cat>.<name>]` layer), at the cost of losing the ability to share named configs across accounts.

Stick with names-as-pointers, or collapse to literal values?

content.text:
Right, that reads better. `repo` should be the parent since `category` is a repo concept, not an account concept.

Updated schema with that reordering:

```toml
[default]
account = "home"
debug = "trace"

# Account "home" — repo defaults
[account.home.repo]
default = "remote"           # default category when --repo is absent

[account.home.repo.category]
remote = "github-personal"   # default name when --repo remote (no =name)
local  = "test-fixtures"     # default name when --repo local (no =name)

# Account "work" — repo defaults
[account.work.repo]
default = "remote"

[account.work.repo.category]
remote = "github-work"
local  = "work-fixtures"

# Named repo configs — account-shared, referenced by [account.*.repo.category]
[repo.remote.github-personal]
provider = "github"
owner    = "winksaville"
service  = "github.com"

[repo.remote.github-work]
provider = "github"
owner    = "anthropic"
service  = "github.com"

[repo.local.test-fixtures]
parent = "~/test-fixtures"

[repo.local.work-fixtures]
parent = "/work/test-fixtures"
```

**Resolution at each level:**
- `[account.<acct>.repo].default` — absolute default category (used when `--repo` absent).
- `[account.<acct>.repo.category].<cat>` — default name within `<cat>` (used when `--repo <cat>` no name).
- `[repo.<cat>.<name>]` — the actual config block.

**`category` is now used two ways:**
- As a *section name* (`[account.<acct>.repo.category]`) — the per-category-defaults table.
- As a *concept* (the keys inside that table — `remote`, `local`, etc.).

That's standard TOML: section names can be common nouns (e.g., Cargo's `[dependencies]` uses "dependencies" both as a section name and a concept).

Lock in?

content: Designing the user-config schema for 0.41.1-4 init reshape: multi-account, named-config repo selection via `--repo cat=name` with category/name defaults. Next: confirm the latest schema (account → repo → category) and start the init.rs reshape implementation.

message.content: How do you describe `--repo remote=github.com` and `--repo local=~/test-fixtures`?

content.text:
Honest answer: **under the names-as-pointers schema, those two CLI invocations don't work as you wrote them.**

- `--repo remote=github.com` → would look up `[repo.remote."github.com"]` (TOML needs the dots quoted). The section has to exist; "github.com" alone isn't enough info to make a URL — init still needs `provider`/`owner`/`service` from somewhere.
- `--repo local=~/test-fixtures` → would look up `[repo.local."~/test-fixtures"]`, which is essentially impossible as a section name (tilde, slash, etc.).

So you're not actually wanting names. You're wanting **literal values** at the CLI — the value after `=` IS the target (URL/path), not a name. Reading (ii).

Under literal-values, the schema collapses considerably and your examples work as-typed:

```toml
[default]
account = "home"

[account.home.repo]
default = "remote"

[account.home.repo.category]
remote = "git@github.com:winksaville"   # URL prefix; init appends /<NAME>.git
local  = "~/test-fixtures"              # fixture parent dir

[account.work.repo]
default = "remote"

[account.work.repo.category]
remote = "git@github.com:anthropic"
local  = "/work/fixtures"
```

CLI invocations:
- `vc-x1 init tf1` → home / remote → `git@github.com:winksaville/tf1.git`
- `vc-x1 init tf1 --repo local` → home / local → bares at `~/test-fixtures/remote-{code,claude}.git`
- `vc-x1 init tf1 --repo remote=git@gitlab.com:winksaville` → literal URL prefix wins
- `vc-x1 init tf1 --repo local=~/scratch` → literal path wins
- `vc-x1 init tf1 --account work --repo remote` → work / remote → `git@github.com:anthropic/tf1.git`

**Trade-off:** literal values are simpler and CLI-symmetric, but the named-`[repo.<cat>.<name>]` layer goes away. Multi-account still works (each account has its own category-keyed defaults). The values themselves carry the structure (URL prefix, path) instead of being looked up.

Drop the `[repo.<cat>.<name>]` layer and go with literal values?

message.content: That feels better but is there a way to not repeat yourself so much i.e. account is listed  4 times but there are only two accounts?

lastPrompt: That feels better but is there a way to not repeat yourself so much i.e. account is listed  4 times but there are only two accounts?

content.text:
Yes — TOML supports dotted keys inside a section, which lets each account become one block:

```toml
[default]
account = "home"
debug   = "trace"

[account.home]
repo.default          = "remote"
repo.category.remote  = "git@github.com:winksaville"
repo.category.local   = "~/test-fixtures"

[account.work]
repo.default          = "remote"
repo.category.remote  = "git@github.com:anthropic"
repo.category.local   = "/work/fixtures"
```

Each account name appears **once** (in its section header). Down from 4 mentions to 2.

Resolution is unchanged — toml_simple flattens dotted keys, so `account.home.repo.category.remote` is the same lookup key as before; we just spelled it more compactly.

Could go further with **shared defaults**:
```toml
[default]
account = "home"

[default.account]
repo.default = "remote"   # all accounts inherit unless overridden

[account.home]
repo.category.remote = "git@github.com:winksaville"
repo.category.local  = "~/test-fixtures"

[account.work]
repo.category.remote = "git@github.com:anthropic"
repo.category.local  = "/work/fixtures"
```

But that adds an inheritance/merge layer in the loader. I'd hold that for a future cycle — for now the compact form (no inheritance) is the lowest-cost step that gets you to "each account stated once."

Lock in the compact form?

message.content: What about using Array Tables would that reduce duplication, https://toml.io/en/v1.0.0#array-of-tables combined with dotted names to nest them?

content.text:
Honest answer: array of tables wouldn't reduce duplication for our case, and it makes things harder.

**With `[[account]]`:**
```toml
[[account]]
name = "home"
repo.default          = "remote"
repo.category.remote  = "git@github.com:winksaville"
repo.category.local   = "~/test-fixtures"

[[account]]
name = "work"
repo.default          = "remote"
repo.category.remote  = "git@github.com:anthropic"
repo.category.local   = "/work/fixtures"
```

vs. **named-section + dotted keys (current):**
```toml
[account.home]
repo.default          = "remote"
repo.category.remote  = "git@github.com:winksaville"
repo.category.local   = "~/test-fixtures"
```

Per-account line count is essentially identical. The array form just trades the section header `[account.home]` for `[[account]]` + a `name = "home"` line — same character count, different shape.

**Two trade-offs that go the wrong way:**

1. **Lookup cost.** `[account.home]` keys directly into `account.home.*`. With `[[account]]`, lookup by name requires scanning the array (`find where name == "home"`). For a config with 2-5 accounts it doesn't matter performance-wise, but the code path is more complex.
2. **Parser cost.** Our `toml_simple` parser doesn't handle `[[...]]` (array of tables) syntax — it strips one layer of brackets, so `[[account]]` would parse as section literal `[account]`. Adding array-of-tables support is a meaningful extension (~30 LOC + tests).

Array of tables shines when entries are **homogeneous list items** (e.g., `[[server]]` for a list of unnamed servers in deployment config). For our case (named accounts looked up by key), `[account.<name>]` is the natural fit.

Recommendation: stick with the dotted-keys form we have. Add `[[...]]` support when we actually have a list-of-unnamed-things use case.

Lock in?

- Shift -5 → -6 (POR detection), -6 → -7 (Fixture migration).

I lean **(B)** — schema changes are easier to review on their own, and -3's existing tests give us a clear before/after diff for the rewrite. Init reshape is plenty for one commit on its own.

file.content:
(PorJjGit, PorGitOnly auto-bootstrap, PorWithPeerPor
  config-only).
- `-6` — `test_helpers::Fixture` migration; audit
  downstream callers across the test suite.
- final — cycle close-out: fill in Example Layout outputs,
  address `notes/vc-x1-init.md` cosmetic anomalies, drop
  the `-N` suffix.

### User config (0.41.1-3)

Bare-NAME init (`vc-x1 init tf1`) needs a place to look up
the implicit owner. Adopt a user-global config at
`~/.config/vc-x1/config.toml` (XDG-compliant; honors
`$XDG_CONFIG_HOME`). No magic fallbacks: missing file or
missing key → predictable error pointing at the config.

Schema:

```toml
[default]
repo-remote-provider = "github"
repo-local-fixtures = "~/test-fixtures"   # optional

[github]
owner = "winksaville"
service = "github.com"   # optional, defaults to "github.com"
```

`[default].repo-remote-provider` selects the per-provider
section (`[github]`, future `[gitlab]`, ...) consulted when
expanding bare NAME for the standard remote-backed init
path. Forward-compatible with the multi-service direction
without restructuring.

`[default].repo-local-fixtures` is the default parent dir
for `vc-x1 init --fixture` when no `[PATH]` is given.
Independent of the provider chain.

Resolution rules (no magic):

- Bare alphanumeric NAME + no
  `[default].repo-remote-provider` set → error: "set
  `[default].repo-remote-provider` in
  `~/.config/vc-x1/config.toml` or use `owner/name`
  shorthand".
- `repo-remote-provider = "github"` + no `[github].owner`
  → error: "set `[github].owner` in
  `~/.config/vc-x1/config.toml`".
- `[github].service` unset → defaults to `"github.com"`
  (canonical host, not magic).
- All present → `tf1` resolves to
  `git@<service>:<owner>/<NAME>.git`.

`--fixture` resolution chain (independent of provider):

- `--fixture <PATH>` → use `<PATH>`.
- `--fixture` (no path) → use
  `[default].repo-local-fixtures`.
- Neither → error: "set `[default].repo-local-fixtures`
  or pass `--fixture <PATH>`".
- `--fixture` is incompatible with URL or `owner/name`
  TARGETs (those dictate a remote); fine with Path or
  bare NAME.

Per-workspace `.vc-config.toml` override (ancestor walk)
is deferred — chicken-and-egg at init time, and the
global file covers the dominant use case.

`-3` lands the config module standalone (`src/config.rs`,
`UserConfig` struct, `load()` / `load_from(path)`); `-4`
wires it into init's bare-NAME path and adds
`--fixture [PATH]`.

#### Refactoring opportunities (post-0.41.1)

input.new_string:
### User config (0.41.1-3, redesigned in 0.41.1-4)

User-global config at `~/.config/vc-x1/config.toml`
(XDG-compliant; honors `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME`). Backs init's
account- and repo-target resolution. No magic fallbacks:
missing file or missing key → predictable error pointing
at the exact key to set.

`-3` shipped a flat first-cut schema (`[default]
repo-remote-provider`, `[github] owner`); `-4` replaces it
with the account/category structure below before init
becomes the first consumer in `-5`. Since `-3` had no
consumers, the rewrite is non-breaking.

Schema:

```toml
[default]
account = "home"      # default --account when absent
debug   = "trace"     # default --debug value when used without arg

[account.home]
repo.default          = "remote"                       # default --repo cat when absent
repo.category.remote  = "git@github.com:winksaville"   # value for --repo remote (no =val)
repo.category.local   = "~/test-fixtures"              # value for --repo local (no =val)

[account.work]
repo.default          = "remote"
repo.category.remote  = "git@github.com:anthropic"
repo.category.local   = "/work/fixtures"
```

Three layers — each level has its own default-finding key:

| CLI                       | Step 1: account                | Step 2: category                  | Step 3: value                                    |
|---|---|---|---|
| (no `--repo`/`--account`) | `[default].account`            | `[account.<a>.repo].default`      | `[account.<a>.repo.category].<cat>`              |
| `--account <a>`           | `<a>` (CLI)                    | `[account.<a>.repo].default`      | `[account.<a>.repo.category].<cat>`              |
| `--repo <cat>`            | `[default].account` (or CLI)   | `<cat>` (CLI)                     | `[account.<a>.repo.category].<cat>`              |
| `--repo <cat>=<val>`      | `[default].account` (or CLI)   | `<cat>` (CLI)                     | `<val>` (CLI, literal)                           |

Values are **literal targets**, not section-name
pointers. For `category = "remote"`, the value is a URL
prefix (init appends `/<NAME>.git`); for `category =
"local"`, it's the parent dir for fixture bares. Built-in
categories `remote` and `local` are recognized; any other
category name errors in `-5` (forward-compat: future
cycles can add a `kind` field for user-defined categories).

Resolution errors (each step has its own message):

- Step 1 missing → `no account specified; set [default].account or use --account <name>`.
- Step 2 missing → `no default category for account '<a>'; set [account.<a>.repo].default or use --repo <cat>`.
- Step 3 missing → `no value for --repo <cat> in account '<a>'; set [account.<a>.repo.category].<cat> or use --repo <cat>=<val>`.

Per-workspace `.vc-config.toml` override (ancestor walk)
is deferred — chicken-and-egg at init time, and the
global file covers the dominant use case.

Module shape:

- `pub struct UserConfig { default_account, default_debug, accounts: HashMap<String, AccountConfig> }`.
- `pub struct AccountConfig { repo_default, repo_category: HashMap<String, String> }`.
- `load()` / `load_from(path)` as in `-3`.
- `resolve_repo(account_override, repo_cli) -> (cat, value)` helper for consumers.

`-4` lands the rewritten module standalone; `-5` (init
reshape) is the first consumer.

`--fixture [PATH]` (init only): create local bare origins
under `PATH` (or `[default].repo-local-fixtures` if `PATH`
omitted) instead of using the configured remote provider.

input.new_string:
`--account <name>` (init only): override
`[default].account`. Determines which `[account.<name>]`
section is consulted for `--repo` resolution.

`--repo <cat>[=<val>]` (init only): pick the repo target
for this run.
- `--repo` absent → use account's `repo.default` →
  category's value.
- `--repo <cat>` → use `<cat>`'s configured value.
- `--repo <cat>=<val>` → use literal `<val>`.

Built-in categories: `remote` (value is a URL prefix —
init appends `/<NAME>.git`) and `local` (value is the
parent dir for fixture bare repos at
`<parent>/remote-{code,claude}.git`). Replaces the old
`--owner` / `--repo-local` / `--repo-remote` /
`--fixture` flag set with one unified mechanism.

`--dry-run`: validate the operation as far as possible
without side effects. Print what would be done.

message.content: Is this a valid simplest ~/.config/vc-x1/config.toml ?

content.text:
Depends on what you want defaulted.

**Empty file is valid** — config is fully optional. With no config, every CLI invocation must specify `--repo <cat>=<val>` explicitly.

**Simplest useful config — fully default-driven `vc-x1 init tf1`:**
```toml
[default]
account = "home"

[account.home]
repo.default          = "remote"
repo.category.remote  = "git@github.com:winksaville"
```
5 lines. Lets `vc-x1 init tf1` resolve to `git@github.com:winksaville/tf1.git` with no flags.

**Variations:**
- Drop `repo.default` (4 lines) if you always pass `--repo remote` on the CLI.
- Drop `[default].account` (4 lines) if you always pass `--account home`.
- Add `repo.category.local = "..."` for fixture-mode default.
- Add a second `[account.work]` block for a second account.

So the truly minimal "useful" one is 4-5 lines. Tests cover empty file (valid → no defaults), partial config (some defaults set), and full schema (everything wired).

Want me to ship this and start -5?

message.content: hmm, but if there is only one set of values we shouldn't need [account] at all?

content.text:
Good point — the account layer is pure overhead when you only have one. Two ways to skip it:

**Option 1 — top-level `[repo]` as single-account shorthand:**
```toml
[repo]
default          = "remote"
category.remote  = "git@github.com:winksaville"
category.local   = "~/test-fixtures"
```
3-4 lines for a complete single-account config. Multi-account still uses `[account.<name>]`.

Loader rule: if `[repo.*]` keys exist at top level, they form an implicit "default" account. If `[account.*]` sections exist, they take precedence (multi-account mode); having both would error to keep things unambiguous.

**Option 2 — keep one schema, just default the account name:**
```toml
[account.default]
repo.default          = "remote"
repo.category.remote  = "git@github.com:winksaville"
```
Same line count as Option 1 but with the `[account.default]` boilerplate. Less elegant but uniform.

**Option 1 is what your message implies.** It's a small loader extension — add a `top_level_repo: Option<AccountConfig>` field and check it in `resolve_repo` after `--account` and `[default].account` fail to find anything.

Resolution chain becomes:
1. Account: `--account` CLI → `[default].account` → top-level `[repo]` (no account) → error.
2. Category: `--repo <cat>` → account's `repo.default` → error.
3. Value: `--repo <cat>=<val>` → account's `repo.category.<cat>` → error.

Add Option 1 to the rewrite, or keep -4 strict-multi-account and add the top-level shorthand in a follow-on?

lastPrompt: hmm, but if there is only one set of values we shouldn't need [account] at all?

### User config (0.41.1-3, redesigned in 0.41.1-4)

User-global config at `~/.config/vc-x1/config.toml`
(XDG-compliant; honors `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME`). Backs init's
account- and repo-target resolution. No magic fallbacks:
missing file or missing key → predictable error pointing
at the exact key to set.

`-3` shipped a flat first-cut schema (`[default]
repo-remote-provider`, `[github] owner`); `-4` replaces it
with the account/category structure below before init
becomes the first consumer in `-5`. Since `-3` had no
consumers, the rewrite is non-breaking.

Schema:

```toml
[default]
account = "home"      # default --account when absent
debug   = "trace"     # default --debug value when used without arg

[account.home]
repo.default          = "remote"                       # default --repo cat when absent
repo.category.remote  = "git@github.com:winksaville"   # value for --repo remote (no =val)
repo.category.local   = "~/test-fixtures"              # value for --repo local (no =val)

[account.work]
repo.default          = "remote"

315	```
316	
317	Three layers — each level has its own default-finding key:
318	
319	| CLI                       | Step 1: account                | Step 2: category                  | Step 3: value                                    |
320	|---|---|---|---|
321	| (no `--repo`/`--account`) | `[default].account`            | `[account.<a>.repo].default`      | `[account.<a>.repo.category].<cat>`              |
322	| `--account <a>`           | `<a>` (CLI)                    | `[account.<a>.repo].default`      | `[account.<a>.repo.category].<cat>`              |
323	| `--repo <cat>`            | `[default].account` (or CLI)   | `<cat>` (CLI)                     | `[account.<a>.repo.category].<cat>`              |
324	| `--repo <cat>=<val>`      | `[default].account` (or CLI)   | `<cat>` (CLI)                     | `<val>` (CLI, literal)                           |
325	
326	Values are **literal targets**, not section-name
327	pointers. For `category = "remote"`, the value is a URL
328	prefix (init appends `/<NAME>.git`); for `category =
329	"local"`, it's the parent dir for fixture bares. Built-in
330	categories `remote` and `local` are recognized; any other
331	category name errors in `-5` (forward-compat: future
332	cycles can add a `kind` field for user-defined categories).
333	
334	Resolution errors (each step has its own message):
335	
336	- Step 1 missing → `no account specified; set [default].account or use --account <name>`.
337	- Step 2 missing → `no default category for account '<a>'; set [account.<a>.repo].default or use --repo <cat>`.
338	- Step 3 missing → `no value for --repo <cat> in account '<a>'; set [account.<a>.repo.category].<cat> or use --repo <cat>=<val>`.
339	
340	Per-workspace `.vc-config.toml` override (ancestor walk)
341	is deferred — chicken-and-egg at init time, and the
342	global file covers the dominant use case.
343	
344	Module shape:
345	
346	- `pub struct UserConfig { default_account, default_debug, accounts: HashMap<String, AccountConfig> }`.
347	- `pub struct AccountConfig { repo_default, repo_category: HashMap<String, String> }`.
348	- `load()` / `load_from(path)` as in `-3`.
349	- `resolve_repo(account_override, repo_cli) -> (cat, value)` helper for consumers.
350	
351	`-4` lands the rewritten module standalone; `-5` (init
352	reshape) is the first consumer.
353	
354	#### Refactoring opportunities (post-0.41.1)
355	
356	Two cleanups surfaced while sizing the module that we
357	deliberately deferred to keep `-3` scoped:
358	
359	- **Unify `.vc-config.toml` accessors onto Pattern B.**
360	  The tree has two patterns for reading TOML config:
361	  - Pattern A (`desc_helpers.rs`, `fix_desc.rs`,
362	    `validate_desc.rs`): call site does
363	    `toml_simple::toml_load(path)` and passes the
364	    resulting `HashMap<String, String>` to map-typed
365	    accessor fns (`other_repo_from_config(&map)`,
366	    `ochid_prefix_from_config(&map)`).
367	  - Pattern B (`push::resolve_state_layout`, new
368	    `config::load_from`): function takes a path,
369	    returns a typed struct with the fields it cares
370	    about; conversion baked in.
371	  Pattern B is more discoverable and testable. A
372	  `WorkspaceConfig` struct with `load_from(path)` could
373	  replace the Pattern-A helpers across desc_helpers /
374	  fix_desc / validate_desc. ~50 LOC, mechanical.
375	- **Layered config precedence** — once `WorkspaceConfig`
376	  is typed, layering user → workspace → CLI becomes
377	  natural (workspace can override `[github].owner` for a
378	  specific project). Init can't use this layer because
379	  it runs *before* a workspace exists, but post-init
380	  commands could.
381	
382	Both candidates for the 0.41.2 cycle. See `notes/todo.md`.
383	
384	### Decisions made during design
385	
386	- **Version + cycle line.** This work + the sync `--check`
387	  fix land on the 0.41.x line. Init+clone = 0.41.1. Sync
388	  fix = a separate cycle (likely 0.41.2). Then rebase the
389	  in-flight 0.42.0 work on top of both.

file.content:
```

Three layers — each level has its own default-finding key:

| CLI                       | Step 1: account                | Step 2: category                  | Step 3: value                                    |
|---|---|---|---|
| (no `--repo`/`--account`) | `[default].account`            | `[account.<a>.repo].default`      | `[account.<a>.repo.category].<cat>`              |
| `--account <a>`           | `<a>` (CLI)                    | `[account.<a>.repo].default`      | `[account.<a>.repo.category].<cat>`              |
| `--repo <cat>`            | `[default].account` (or CLI)   | `<cat>` (CLI)                     | `[account.<a>.repo.category].<cat>`              |
| `--repo <cat>=<val>`      | `[default].account` (or CLI)   | `<cat>` (CLI)                     | `<val>` (CLI, literal)                           |

Values are **literal targets**, not section-name
pointers. For `category = "remote"`, the value is a URL
prefix (init appends `/<NAME>.git`); for `category =
"local"`, it's the parent dir for fixture bares. Built-in
categories `remote` and `local` are recognized; any other
category name errors in `-5` (forward-compat: future
cycles can add a `kind` field for user-defined categories).

Resolution errors (each step has its own message):

- Step 1 missing → `no account specified; set [default].account or use --account <name>`.
- Step 2 missing → `no default category for account '<a>'; set [account.<a>.repo].default or use --repo <cat>`.
- Step 3 missing → `no value for --repo <cat> in account '<a>'; set [account.<a>.repo.category].<cat> or use --repo <cat>=<val>`.

Per-workspace `.vc-config.toml` override (ancestor walk)
is deferred — chicken-and-egg at init time, and the
global file covers the dominant use case.

Module shape:

- `pub struct UserConfig { default_account, default_debug, accounts: HashMap<String, AccountConfig> }`.
- `pub struct AccountConfig { repo_default, repo_category: HashMap<String, String> }`.
- `load()` / `load_from(path)` as in `-3`.
- `resolve_repo(account_override, repo_cli) -> (cat, value)` helper for consumers.

`-4` lands the rewritten module standalone; `-5` (init
reshape) is the first consumer.

#### Refactoring opportunities (post-0.41.1)

Two cleanups surfaced while sizing the module that we
deliberately deferred to keep `-3` scoped:

- **Unify `.vc-config.toml` accessors onto Pattern B.**
  The tree has two patterns for reading TOML config:
  - Pattern A (`desc_helpers.rs`, `fix_desc.rs`,
    `validate_desc.rs`): call site does
    `toml_simple::toml_load(path)` and passes the
    resulting `HashMap<String, String>` to map-typed
    accessor fns (`other_repo_from_config(&map)`,
    `ochid_prefix_from_config(&map)`).
  - Pattern B (`push::resolve_state_layout`, new
    `config::load_from`): function takes a path,
    returns a typed struct with the fields it cares
    about; conversion baked in.
  Pattern B is more discoverable and testable. A
  `WorkspaceConfig` struct with `load_from(path)` could
  replace the Pattern-A helpers across desc_helpers /
  fix_desc / validate_desc. ~50 LOC, mechanical.
- **Layered config precedence** — once `WorkspaceConfig`
  is typed, layering user → workspace → CLI becomes
  natural (workspace can override `[github].owner` for a
  specific project). Init can't use this layer because
  it runs *before* a workspace exists, but post-init
  commands could.

Both candidates for the 0.41.2 cycle. See `notes/todo.md`.

### Decisions made during design

- **Version + cycle line.** This work + the sync `--check`
  fix land on the 0.41.x line. Init+clone = 0.41.1. Sync
  fix = a separate cycle (likely 0.41.2). Then rebase the
  in-flight 0.42.0 work on top of both.

input.new_string:
Resolution errors (each step has its own message):

- Step 1 missing → `no account specified; set [default].account, use --account <name>, or write a top-level [repo] section`.
- Step 2 missing → `no default category for account '<a>'; set [account.<a>.repo].default or use --repo <cat>`.
- Step 3 missing → `no value for --repo <cat> in account '<a>'; set [account.<a>.repo.category].<cat> or use --repo <cat>=<val>`.

#### Single-account shorthand: top-level `[repo]`

`[account.<name>]` boilerplate is overhead when there's
only one account. The loader accepts a top-level `[repo]`
section as a single-account shorthand:

```toml
[repo]
default          = "remote"
category.remote  = "git@github.com:winksaville"
category.local   = "~/test-fixtures"
```

3-4 lines for a complete config — no `[default].account`,
no `[account.<name>]`. Resolution: when no `--account` is
given and no `[default].account` is set, the loader uses
the top-level `[repo]` block as the implicit account.

Mixing top-level `[repo]` **and** `[account.*]` sections
is rejected at load time (ambiguous which one init should
consult — error: `mixing top-level [repo] with [account.*]
is ambiguous`).

Top-level error format uses `[repo.category].<cat>` rather
than `[account.<a>.repo.category].<cat>`.

Per-workspace `.vc-config.toml` override (ancestor walk)
is deferred — chicken-and-egg at init time, and the
global file covers the dominant use case.

Module shape:

- `pub struct UserConfig { default_account, default_debug, top_level_repo: Option<AccountConfig>, accounts: HashMap<String, AccountConfig> }`.
- `pub struct AccountConfig { repo_default, repo_category: HashMap<String, String> }`.
- `pub struct RepoSelector { category, value: Option<String> }` — parsed `--repo` form.
- `load()` / `load_from(path)` — load + group dotted keys.
- `resolve_repo(cfg, account_override, repo_cli) -> (cat, value)` — three-step chain.

`-4` lands the rewritten module standalone; `-5` (init
reshape) is the first consumer.

Per-workspace `.vc-config.toml` override (ancestor walk)
is deferred — chicken-and-egg at init time, and the
global file covers the dominant use case.

Module shape:

- `pub struct UserConfig { default_account, default_debug, accounts: HashMap<String, AccountConfig> }`.
- `pub struct AccountConfig { repo_default, repo_category: HashMap<String, String> }`.
- `load()` / `load_from(path)` as in `-3`.
- `resolve_repo(account_override, repo_cli) -> (cat, value)` helper for consumers.

`-4` lands the rewritten module standalone; `-5` (init
reshape) is the first consumer.

Resolution errors (each step has its own message):

- Step 1 missing → `no account specified; set [default].account, use --account <name>, or write a top-level [repo] section`.
- Step 2 missing → `no default category for account '<a>'; set [account.<a>.repo].default or use --repo <cat>`.
- Step 3 missing → `no value for --repo <cat> in account '<a>'; set [account.<a>.repo.category].<cat> or use --repo <cat>=<val>`.

#### Single-account shorthand: top-level `[repo]`

`[account.<name>]` boilerplate is overhead when there's
only one account. The loader accepts a top-level `[repo]`
section as a single-account shorthand:

```toml
[repo]
default          = "remote"
category.remote  = "git@github.com:winksaville"
category.local   = "~/test-fixtures"
```

3-4 lines for a complete config — no `[default].account`,
no `[account.<name>]`. Resolution: when no `--account` is
given and no `[default].account` is set, the loader uses
the top-level `[repo]` block as the implicit account.

Mixing top-level `[repo]` **and** `[account.*]` sections
is rejected at load time (ambiguous which one init should
consult — error: `mixing top-level [repo] with [account.*]
is ambiguous`).

Top-level error format uses `[repo.category].<cat>` rather
than `[account.<a>.repo.category].<cat>`.

Per-workspace `.vc-config.toml` override (ancestor walk)
is deferred — chicken-and-egg at init time, and the
global file covers the dominant use case.

Module shape:

- `pub struct UserConfig { default_account, default_debug, top_level_repo: Option<AccountConfig>, accounts: HashMap<String, AccountConfig> }`.
- `pub struct AccountConfig { repo_default, repo_category: HashMap<String, String> }`.
- `pub struct RepoSelector { category, value: Option<String> }` — parsed `--repo` form.
- `load()` / `load_from(path)` — load + group dotted keys.
- `resolve_repo(cfg, account_override, repo_cli) -> (cat, value)` — three-step chain.

`-4` lands the rewritten module standalone; `-5` (init
reshape) is the first consumer.

attachment.snippet:
1	//! User-level config loaded from `~/.config/vc-x1/config.toml`.
2	//!
3	//! Backs init's account/repo-target resolution. No magic
4	//! fallbacks — a missing file or missing key produces a
5	//! predictable error pointing at the exact key to set.
6	//!
7	//! Schema (multi-account, dotted keys for compactness):
8	//!
9	//! ```toml
10	//! [default]
11	//! account = "home"      # default --account when absent
12	//! debug   = "trace"     # default --debug value when used without arg
13	//!
14	//! [account.home]
15	//! repo.default          = "remote"                       # default --repo cat when absent
16	//! repo.category.remote  = "git@github.com:winksaville"   # value for --repo remote (no =val)
17	//! repo.category.local   = "~/test-fixtures"              # value for --repo local (no =val)
18	//!
19	//! [account.work]
20	//! repo.default          = "remote"
21	//! repo.category.remote  = "git@github.com:anthropic"
22	//! repo.category.local   = "/work/fixtures"
23	//! ```
24	//!
25	//! Three-layer resolution — see
26	//! `notes/chores-08.md > User config (0.41.1-3, redesigned in 0.41.1-4)`:
27	//!
28	//! 1. account: CLI `--account` → `[default].account` → error.
29	//! 2. category: CLI `--repo <cat>` → `[account.<a>.repo].default`
30	//!    → error.
31	//! 3. value: CLI `--repo <cat>=<val>` →
32	//!    `[account.<a>.repo.category].<cat>` → error.
33	//!
34	//! Values are **literal targets**, not section-name pointers.
35	//! For built-in `category = "remote"` the value is a URL prefix
36	//! (init appends `/<NAME>.git`); for `category = "local"` it's
37	//! the parent dir for fixture bare repos.
38	//!
39	//! `-3` shipped a flat first-cut (`[default] repo-remote-provider`,
40	//! `[github] owner`); `-4` is the redesign before any consumer
41	//! is wired in. `-5` (init reshape) is the first consumer.
42	
43	use std::collections::HashMap;
44	use std::path::{Path, PathBuf};
45	
46	use crate::toml_simple;
47	
48	/// Per-account configuration.
49	///
50	/// - `repo_default` — `[account.<name>.repo].default` — the
51	///   category to use when `--repo` is absent.
52	/// - `repo_category` — `[account.<name>.repo.category]` — map
53	///   from category name (e.g. `"remote"`, `"local"`) to its
54	///   literal value (URL prefix, fixture parent dir, etc.).
55	#[derive(Debug, Default, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]
56	pub struct AccountConfig {
57	    pub repo_default: Option<String>,
58	    pub repo_category: HashMap<String, String>,
59	}
60	
61	/// Resolved user config.
62	///
63	/// All fields are optional or default-empty — absent file or
64	/// absent keys yield empty values. Consumers apply their own
65	/// errors at use time.
66	///
67	/// **Two write modes** for repo defaults:
68	/// - **Top-level `[repo]`** — single-account shorthand. Sits in
69	///   `top_level_repo`. Used when neither `--account` nor
70	///   `[default].account` resolves to a known account.
71	/// - **`[account.<name>]`** — multi-account. Sits in `accounts`.
72	///   Selected by `--account` CLI or `[default].account`.
73	///
74	/// Mixing both is rejected at load time (ambiguous which one
75	/// init should consult).
76	#[derive(Debug, Default, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]
77	pub struct UserConfig {
78	    /// `[default].account` — default account when `--account`
79	    /// is absent.
80	    pub default_account: Option<String>,
81	
82	    /// `[default].debug` — default value when `--debug` is used
83	    /// without an argument. (Reserved; not currently consumed.)
84	    pub default_debug: Option<String>,
85	
86	    /// Top-level `[repo]` — single-account shorthand. `Some`
87	    /// only when the file has top-level `repo.*` keys and no
88	    /// `[account.*]` sections.
89	    pub top_level_repo: Option<AccountConfig>,
90	
91	    /// Per-account configuration, keyed by account name.
92	    pub accounts: HashMap<String, AccountConfig>,
93	}
94	
95	/// CLI selector for `--repo <cat>[=<val>]`.
96	///
97	/// - `--repo` absent → `None`.
98	/// - `--repo <cat>` → `Some({ category: cat, value: None })`.
99	/// - `--repo <cat>=<val>` → `Some({ category: cat, value: Some(val) })`.
100	#[allow(dead_code)] // OK: 0.41.1-5 is the first consumer; staged ahead in -4.
101	#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]
102	pub struct RepoSelector {
103	    pub category: String,
104	    pub value: Option<String>,
105	}
106	
107	/// Path to the user config file.
108	///
109	/// - Honors `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME` if set (XDG Base Directory spec).
110	/// - Falls back to `$HOME/.config`.
111	/// - Errors if neither variable is set.
112	pub fn config_path() -> Result<PathBuf, Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
113	    let base = if let Some(xdg) = std::env::var_os("XDG_CONFIG_HOME") {
114	        PathBuf::from(xdg)
115	    } else if let Some(home) = std::env::var_os("HOME") {
116	        PathBuf::from(home).join(".config")
117	    } else {
118	        return Err("neither $XDG_CONFIG_HOME nor $HOME is set".into());
119	    };
120	    Ok(base.join("vc-x1").join("config.toml"))
121	}
122	
123	/// Load the user config from the default location.
124	///
125	/// - Missing file → empty `UserConfig`.
126	/// - Malformed file → fatal error.
127	#[allow(dead_code)] // OK: 0.41.1-5 is the first consumer; staged ahead in -4.
128	pub fn load() -> Result<UserConfig, Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
129	    load_from(&config_path()?)
130	}
131	
132	/// Load the user config from an explicit path.
133	///
134	/// - Missing file → empty `UserConfig`.
135	/// - Malformed file → fatal error (propagated from `toml_simple`).
136	/// - Unknown sections / keys are silently ignored
137	///   (forward-compatible with future schema additions).
138	pub fn load_from(path: &Path) -> Result<UserConfig, Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
139	    if !path.exists() {
140	        return Ok(UserConfig::default());
141	    }
142	    let map = toml_simple::toml_load(path)?;
143	
144	    let mut cfg = UserConfig {
145	        default_account: map.get("default.account").cloned(),
146	        default_debug: map.get("default.debug").cloned(),
147	        top_level_repo: None,
148	        accounts: HashMap::new(),
149	    };
150	
151	    let mut top_level = AccountConfig::default();
152	    let mut top_level_seen = false;
153	
154	    // Walk all keys; route `repo.*` to top-level, `account.<n>.…`
155	    // to per-account.
156	    for (full_key, value) in &map {
157	        if let Some(suffix) = full_key.strip_prefix("repo.") {
158	            top_level_seen = true;
159	            apply_repo_subkey(&mut top_level, suffix, value);
160	            continue;
161	        }
162	        let Some(rest) = full_key.strip_prefix("account.") else {
163	            continue;
164	        };
165	        let Some((name, suffix)) = rest.split_once('.') else {
166	            continue;
167	        };
168	        let Some(repo_suffix) = suffix.strip_prefix("repo.") else {
169	            continue; // unknown sub-section under [account.<n>]
170	        };
171	        let account = cfg
172	            .accounts
173	            .entry(name.to_string())
174	            .or_insert_with(AccountConfig::default);
175	        apply_repo_subkey(account, repo_suffix, value);
176	    }
177	
178	    if top_level_seen && !cfg.accounts.is_empty() {
179	        return Err(format!(
180	            "{}: mixing top-level [repo] with [account.*] is ambiguous — \
181	             remove top-level [repo] or move it under [account.<name>]",
182	            path.display()
183	        )
184	        .into());
185	    }
186	    if top_level_seen {
187	        cfg.top_level_repo = Some(top_level);
188	    }
189	
190	    Ok(cfg)
191	}
192	
193	/// Apply one `repo.*` sub-key (e.g. `default`, `category.remote`)
194	/// onto an `AccountConfig`. Used for both the top-level `[repo]`
195	/// path and the `[account.<n>.repo]` path.
196	fn apply_repo_subkey(account: &mut AccountConfig, suffix: &str, value: &str) {
197	    match suffix {
198	        "default" => {
199	            account.repo_default = Some(value.to_string());
200	        }
201	        s if let Some(cat) = s.strip_prefix("category.") => {
202	            account
203	                .repo_category
